title: Spawn flow creates warren run row before burrow provisioning, attaches IDs as each call returns
rationale: The §4.3 composition flow has two HTTP boundaries (POST /burrows then POST /burrows/:id/runs). Creating the warren run_xxx id first lets log lines, error messages, and event payloads reference it throughout the flow without a chicken-and-egg between warren and burrow IDs. attachBurrow was widened to accept partial inputs so each ID can land independently; failure path runs finalize(cancelled) + best-effort burrow.destroy().

title: Spawn reads agent definition from agents table cache, does not re-render via cn at spawn time
rationale: Re-rendering on every spawn would shell out to cn, adding latency to a request-driven path. Operators control freshness via POST /agents/refresh. The cached row already represents a recent cn render output that survived schema validation. SPEC §4.3's 'frozen for the lifetime of the run' requirement is satisfied by snapshotting the cache onto runs.rendered_agent_json at spawn.

content: Burrow workspace seeding writes .canopy/agent.json (full AgentDefinition envelope), .mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl (one append per expertise_seed line, grouped by domain), and .seeds/workflow.txt (workflow body verbatim). Deliberately a single agent.json rather than per-section files since harness file-layout expectations are not yet locked — the harness reads whichever sections it needs.

title: burrow_config TOML parsing uses in-house line extractor, not smol-toml
rationale: HttpBurrowUpInput only forwards [sandbox].network onto POST /burrows; the rest of an agent's burrow_config (toolchain, allowed_domains) lives in the burrow profile and isn't exposed via the HTTP route. Adding a TOML parser dependency just to read one quoted-string field would be heavy. A line-based state machine with quoted-string + comment handling is sufficient and stays deterministic on the small, hand-authored configs these are. Swap to smol-toml (already in burrow-cli's tree) if/when the surface grows.

name: spawnRun rollback on failure
description: Any failure between POST /burrows and the final attachBurrow runs through a single try/catch that: (1) finalizes the warren run as 'cancelled' (queued → cancelled is allowed by ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS), and (2) best-effort calls burrow.destroy() if the burrow was provisioned. The original error is rethrown — cleanup never masks the caller-facing diagnostic. Both cleanup steps swallow their own errors so a stale db handle or unreachable burrow doesn't trade one error for another.

content: RunsRepo.attachBurrow accepts partial inputs (burrowId-only, burrowRunId-only, or both). The §4.3 flow attaches each ID separately as it returns from burrow. The repo throws ValidationError if neither field is set so accidental no-op calls fail loudly.

title: Resume-seq dedup happens client-side, not via ?since= on /runs/:id/stream
rationale: Burrow's GET /runs/:id/stream does not accept a ?since= query parameter — only the burrow-scoped /burrows/:id/events route does. The seed (warren-26d2) explicitly says 'restart-recovery at MAX(seq)+1', so warren computes maxSeq via EventsRepo.maxSeqForRun(runId) and drops incoming events whose seq <= maxSeq on the consumer side. Cost: a brief replay of already-persisted events on every subscribe; benefit: no schema/wire change needed and the bridge stays a pure courier.

content: bridgeRunStream writes the row to the events table FIRST, then calls broker.publish(runId, row). This ordering matters for tailRunEvents: a route handler that subscribes-then-snapshots can't see an event the bridge persisted but hasn't yet broadcast (it'll be in the snapshot); it can't see one published-but-not-yet-persisted (publish only happens after append). The seam dedup in tailRunEvents relies on this invariant.

name: tailRunEvents replay-then-live with seam dedup
description: The HTTP /runs/:id/events?follow=1 helper subscribes to the broker BEFORE reading history from EventsRepo.listByRun. It then yields history first (tracking lastYielded seq), then yields live events while skipping any whose burrow_event_seq <= lastYielded. Subscribing-after-snapshot naively would drop events committed in the gap; subscribing-before with no dedup would duplicate the seam events. Both ends are required.

title: bridgeRunStream is a pure event courier — no run state mirroring
rationale: Burrow's per-run lifecycle (queued → running → terminal) is observed by Phase 7 (reap). The bridge does not call repos.runs.markRunning or finalize; it only writes events and publishes them. Keeping bridge concerns narrow lets it run idempotently across restarts (no state machine guards to worry about) and keeps the reap orchestration (close seeds, push branch, copy mulch) in one place. The seed warren-26d2 scope is explicit: 'subscribe, write-through, fan out, restart-recovery'.

title: Reap LWW key is record `recorded_at`, not the spec's generic "ts"
rationale: SPEC §11.A says 'last-write-wins by record ts'; the canonical mulch field is recorded_at (set by ml record). String-comparing ISO8601 timestamps gives correct ordering without parsing.

content: Warren-emitted (non-burrow) events use stream='system' and burrow_event_seq starting at MAX(seq for run) + 1, so reap.completed/mulch.record.*/reap_failed events sort after bridge-written events in /runs/:id/events?follow=1.

name: Reap step error isolation
description: Each reap sub-step (workspace_lookup, mulch_merge, seeds_close, branch_push) wraps in try/catch and emits a reap_failed event with the step name; the warren state-transition (queued/running → terminal) runs unconditionally afterward, so a sub-step failure cannot leave the row stuck in running. Per SPEC §11.A: reap errors do not fail the run.

title: Reap bridges queued→running before queued→terminal
rationale: RunsRepo's state machine forbids queued→succeeded/failed directly (queued can only go to running or cancelled). reapRun.transitionToTerminal calls markRunning(now) when current is queued and outcome is succeeded|failed, then finalize. queued→cancelled is direct (legal transition). Idempotent against runs already terminal: short-circuits with alreadyTerminal=true.

title: cancelRun forwards to burrow without transitioning warren state
rationale: Reap (Phase 7) is the only path that finalizes a non-terminal warren run, because the terminal transition is paired with mulch merge, seeds-close mirror, and branch push. If cancelRun transitioned state directly, reap's isTerminal short-circuit would skip those sub-steps and the operator would silently lose the agent's partial work. The cancel pipeline is: warren cancelRun -> burrow cancels -> burrow emits run terminal event -> bridgeRunStream persists -> reap fires with outcome=cancelled.

content: steerRun requires only burrow_id, not burrow_run_id — the burrow inbox is per-burrow (POST /burrows/:id/inbox), so a queued warren run that has been provisioned (burrow_id set) but not yet dispatched (burrow_run_id null) is still steerable. The message is delivered to the next agent turn on that burrow.

cancelRun has a partial-spawn bypass: when state=queued and burrow_run_id is null, warren transitions queued -> cancelled directly without a wire call (there is no remote run to cancel). Other state combinations with burrow_run_id null are rejected as ValidationError because they shouldn't be reachable through the spawn flow.

name: control-plane operations emit a warren-side audit event
description: Control-plane operations on a run (steer, cancel) emit a warren-emitted system event into the run's event log: steer.sent for steerRun, cancel.requested for cancelRun. They use stream='system' and seq=MAX(seq for run) + 1 (matching the existing reap convention), and publish through the optional broker so live tailers see the operator action inline with burrow's own event stream. The event is only written after the wire call succeeds — failures (validation, transport, BurrowError) leave the event log untouched.

content: Warren's canopy agent.name is the burrow runtime id by convention (e.g., 'claude-code' → claude-code runtime). spawn.ts forwards [agent.name] as HttpBurrowUpInput.agents so burrow can mount the runtime's binary into the sandbox even when the project clone has no burrow.toml. If a future agent decouples the canopy name from the runtime id, plumb a separate runtimeId field through registry/schema.ts and switch the forward source.

name: spawnRun forwards agent identity at up-time
description: provisionBurrow passes agents: [agent.name] in HttpBurrowUpInput so burrow's collectToolchainPaths sees an effective [[agents]] row even when the project has no burrow.toml. The seam is HTTP only — burrow's BurrowsClient.up forwards options.agents into runUpCommand, which merges into burrowToml.agents before profile build. Without this forward, claude-code spawns hit 'bwrap: execvp claude: No such file or directory' (warren-8526 / burrow-55e3).

content: warren's spawn flow forwards agent.name (the canopy prompt name) to burrow as the 'agentId' in POST /burrows/:id/runs. burrow looks up agentId in its runtime registry to pick an executor. Built-in runtimes are 'claude-code', 'sapling', 'codex' (see burrow's knownBuiltInIds). A canopy prompt tagged 'agent' that should drive claude-code MUST be named 'claude-code' — naming it 'hello-bot' or anything else makes burrow accept the run but never execute it (no registered runtime), and the warren reap immediately marks it failed even though branchPushed:true and errors:[]. Custom runtimes can be registered via burrow.toml [[agents]] but the default path is name = built-in id.

spawnRun's projectsConfig + projectSpawn are paired opt-in: when both are wired (production via createRunHandler), the project clone is refreshed via refreshProject() BEFORE provisionBurrow so agents see up-to-date code. When either is omitted, refresh is skipped (back-compat for unit tests that pre-stage their own fixtures). An optional body.ref field on POST /runs forwards through to refreshProject as the target branch/tag/SHA. The refresh runs before the warren run row is created so a fetch failure does not strand a queued row.

name: bridge-claims-queued-running
description: bridgeRunStream calls repos.runs.claimById(runId) on the first observed event, atomically transitioning queued→running so HTTP clients polling /runs/:id stop seeing 'queued' while the agent is actively working. The claim is idempotent (returns null if already running/terminal) and runs once per bridge invocation via a local 'claimed' flag. Reap still owns terminal transitions and its existing queued→running shim (transitionToTerminal in reap.ts) becomes a no-op when the bridge already claimed.

title: Bridge mirrors queued→running but reap still owns terminal transitions
rationale: Original posture (mx-5157ab) made bridge a pure event courier so reap was the sole writer to runs.state — but reap only fires on burrow terminal, leaving runs stuck on 'queued' in the UI for the entire active lifetime (warren-865e). Splitting the responsibility is the smallest fix: bridge claims queued→running on first event (cheap, idempotent), reap finalizes terminal. Keeps reap's special-case bridging through queued→running intact for runs that go straight to terminal without ever streaming an event.

name: reap-failure-cause-discriminator
description: When reap finalizes a run with outcome=failed, it records a failure_reason on runs to distinguish 'burrow accepted dispatch but never started' from 'agent ran and crashed'. Inference rule: warren state on entry to reap — queued ⇒ never_started (bridge never claimed because no events ever flowed from burrow), running ⇒ crashed (bridge claimed on a real event). ReapRunInput.failureReason overrides inference; reserved 'timed_out' for a future deadline-based reaper. Persisted on runs.failure_reason (TEXT, nullable, only when state=failed), echoed in the reap.completed event payload, mirrored in ReapRunResult, and shown next to StateBadge on RunDetail.

name: branchPushed-requires-both-reap-and-sandbox-git
description: An agent run produces a remote branch (branchPushed:true) only when TWO independent things both work: (1) the agent can run git commit inside the sandbox — requires the burrow worktree's gitdir reference to be reachable from inside bwrap (warren-67cc), and (2) warren's reap step transitions the run AND runs git push for the burrow branch (warren-a69a). When debugging 'agent ran successfully but no branch on remote', check both: peek the burrow worktree for commits AND check whether reap fired. Either failing alone produces the same observable shape (run completes inside the sandbox, warren UI says 'running' forever, no remote branch). First dogfood (warren-5h ago) had both working; second dogfood (run_rpe5tvs06bpg, 2026-05-09) had both broken — net: 12m48s of real, validated work (417/417 tests, lint+typecheck green) but zero output landed on the remote.

content: runs.projectId is nullable (string | null) on RunRow — null encodes 'project was deleted while this run existed', a state introduced by the ON DELETE SET NULL FK shape (warren-5f19). reap.ts must guard run.projectId === null before calling repos.projects.get(); when null, mulch_merge / seeds_close / branch_push are skipped because the project clone on disk is gone, but the state transition (queued/running → terminal) still runs so warren rows never strand in 'running'. UI surfaces this as '(deleted project)' on Runs list + RunDetail.

name: bridge-detects-runtime-terminal
description: bridgeRunStream inspects each persisted event for a runtime-terminal shape (kind=state_change, stream=system, payload.type=result for claude-code) and breaks the loop with terminalDetected={outcome} set on the result. The registry layer (server/bridges.ts runWithReconnect) reads terminalDetected and calls reapRun inline. Without this, burrow's infinite poll-based run-stream never auto-closes, so warren runs would stay 'running' forever after the agent exited (warren-a69a). Bridge still does NOT transition warren state directly — reap owns that, consistent with mx-fadaa2.

title: cancelRun reaps inline when burrow returns terminal state
rationale: Burrow's cancel response already carries the post-cancel run state. When that state is in {succeeded,failed,cancelled} (the typical case for a graceful cancel), waiting for an external reap scheduler leaves the warren row stuck in 'running' indefinitely (warren-a69a — there is no separate reap scheduler in the HTTP server build, only the CLI command had reap wired). cancelRun now calls reapRun inline using burrowRun.state as the outcome. Reap is best-effort and idempotent, so failures land on the run's event log rather than escaping the cancel response. This matches the bridge-terminal short-circuit: both paths trigger reap synchronously when they observe terminal burrow state, eliminating the dependency on a poll-based reap scheduler for the common cases.

content: Burrow's GET /runs/:id/stream is an infinite poll loop (events table tail with default 200ms poll interval) and does NOT auto-close when the burrow run reaches a terminal state. Warren's bridgeRunStream therefore cannot rely on natural source-end as the terminal signal — without an in-stream terminal detector or external short-circuit, the bridge polls forever and warren's run state never transitions out of 'running'.

name: no-model-response-discriminator
description: Reap distinguishes 'agent never produced model output' from generic crashed by checking the events table for kind in {text,thinking,tool_use} on stream=stdout. burrow's jsonl-claude parser maps every claude-code assistant content block into one of those three kinds, so absence of all three = no assistant turn ran. Used to surface credential/auth failures (warren-5165 root case: claude-code emits init system event, prints 'Not logged in', exits — no assistant turn) without a brittle stderr regex.

name: acceptance-canopy-mutation-via-cn-update
description: Acceptance scenarios verify spawn-time freeze of runs.rendered_agent_json by: (a) POST /runs to capture a baseline run, (b) running 'cn update <name> --section system --body <drift>' in the canopy source repo (ctx.fixtures.canopyRepoPath) followed by 'cn sync' (or git add+commit fallback), (c) POST /agents/refresh to make warren re-clone+re-render, (d) GET /runs/:id to confirm the old run's renderedAgentJson is unchanged while a fresh POST /runs reflects the drift. ScenarioCtx.fixtures.canopyRepoPath was added so scenarios can drive this mutation; the existing canopyRepoUrl alone is insufficient (it points to the fake github.com URL, not the on-disk source).

name: branchPushed-true-without-commit
description: branchPushed: true is silent on whether the agent committed anything. src/runs/reap.ts:257-265 pushes HEAD without checking commitsAhead; runs where the agent never ran git commit produce branch refs at the base SHA and reap reports success. Witnessed live in run_a98cfx1fantf (2026-05-09): burrow/bur_r9mjn6da9xc9 at main's SHA, ahead_by 0. Operator confirmation bias is real — a branch in 'gh' listing reads as success even though gh compare main...<branch> shows total_commits: 0. Distinguishable run shape (real success: run_agpet4ev6e4a, ahead_by 1) is invisible in the UI without explicit commits-ahead surfacing. Tracked as warren-f3bb (P1).

title: warren prepends agent.sections.system to dispatch prompt
rationale:
Burrow's claude-code runtime (burrow/src/runtime/claude-code.ts:171-188) only feeds ctx.prompt and pendingMessages into claude's stdin — it never reads .canopy/agent.json, so the canopy 'system' body was dead text on disk. composeDispatchPrompt(systemBody, userPrompt) joins them as 'system

---

userPrompt' and spawnRun passes the composed body to dispatchRun. runs.prompt (warren-side) keeps the user-typed input verbatim; only the body sent on POST /burrows/:id/runs is composed. Empty/whitespace system falls back to userPrompt unchanged. Closes warren-1a09 fix A loading mechanism: the commit-only contract baked into canopy claude-code v3 now actually reaches the agent.

name: reap-empty-push-discriminator
description: Reap distinguishes a real-work push from a no-op push (warren-f3bb): after exit-0 from 'git push', it runs 'git rev-list --count <project.defaultBranch>..HEAD' inside the burrow workspace and pins the result to ReapRunResult.commitsAhead (number | null). When commitsAhead === 0, reap emits a system event 'reap.empty_push' carrying { branch, baseBranch, message }. The reap.completed payload also carries commitsAhead so any consumer (UI, audits, downstream automation) can tell the silent twin (agent never committed; branchPushed:true against unchanged HEAD) apart from a real shipped run. Base ref comes from project.defaultBranch (warren's projects table, notNull) — not from BurrowRow, which doesn't expose baseBranch at the top level (it lives in providerStateJson). rev-list failures or a missing baseBranch degrade to commitsAhead:null (non-fatal, no reap_failed step). UI's RunDetail surfaces a destructive 'empty push' badge when branchPushed && commitsAhead===0 and a green '+N commits' badge when commitsAhead>0; both read the latest reap.completed event off the stream because the run row itself doesn't store this field.

content: Reap chooses base ref for the empty-push count from project.defaultBranch (warren-f3bb), NOT burrow.baseBranch. BurrowRow has no baseBranch top-level field — it's stored inside providerStateJson — and warren's projects table already pins defaultBranch (notNull) at clone time. Acceptable trade-off for V1: when an agent runs against a non-default base, commitsAhead would be wrong. If/when burrow exposes baseBranch on its HTTP surface, swap the source.

name: reap-pr-open-step
description: Reap's 4th best-effort sub-step opens a GitHub PR after branch_push (warren-f6af). Gated by WARREN_AUTO_OPEN_PR (default on) + GITHUB_TOKEN + outcome=succeeded + branchPushed + commitsAhead>0 + branch != project.defaultBranch. Calls POST /repos/:owner/:repo/pulls via injected fetch (no gh shell-out, no extra dep). 422 'already exists' is treated as success: GET /pulls?head=owner:branch&base=default to recover the existing url. Persisted on runs.pr_url and surfaced on reap.completed.prUrl + a reap.pr_opened system event. Failures emit reap_failed step=pr_open without failing the run.

title: Use REST POST /pulls via injected fetch, not gh shell-out, for warren-f6af
rationale: Codebase already prefers injected fetch/spawn seams for testability (BurrowClient, registry/projects SpawnFn). Adding gh as a runtime dep just for one call would balloon the Dockerfile and require shelling out under bwrap-friendly flags. Direct POST /repos/:owner/:repo/pulls with GITHUB_TOKEN (already plumbed by the supervisor's git insteadOf rewrite, mx-related to warren-dcf3) gives finer-grained 422-vs-other error handling and uses the same auth path as git push.

content: WARREN_AUTO_OPEN_PR (warren-f6af) defaults to enabled; loadAutoOpenPrConfigFromEnv treats only {0,false,no,off,empty} (case+whitespace-insensitive) as falsy, so any unrecognized value (including 'true', 'yes', 'always') leaves it on. Loaded once at boot in src/server/main.ts and threaded through ServerDeps.autoOpenPr → cancelRun → reap, and in src/cli/commands/run.ts via deps.autoOpenPr.

name: acceptance-restart-recovery
description: scenario 06 verifies SPEC §9 MAX(seq)+1 restart-recovery: spawn long-running run, wait for ≥3 events to land in warren's events table, killWarren via ScenarioLifecycle hook (burrow stays up; agent keeps emitting), restartWarren (bootBridges resumes the bridge for queued/running rows with non-null burrow_run_id), wait for max seq to grow past pre-kill, assert no gaps in seqs and that every pre-kill seq is still present. The stub agent's per-second heartbeat loop (lib/stub-agent/agent.sh) is what gives the bridge a steady stream of new burrow events during the warren-down window — without per-second heartbeats the recovery path is silently dormant (no new events to bridge after restart, so a broken recovery looks identical to a working one). Cancel run in finally so teardown doesn't race the 8s sleep.

content: Acceptance harness boots burrow via scripts/acceptance/lib/burrow-serve.ts, NOT the 'burrow' CLI. burrow's AgentRegistry only seeds BUILT_IN_RUNTIMES (claude-code, sapling, codex); declarative [[agents]] in a project's burrow.toml feed toolchainPath/credentialPath collection but are NEVER auto-registered with the in-memory runtime registry (resolveEffectiveAgents in node_modules/@os-eco/burrow-cli/src/cli/commands/up.ts). The shim does Client.open() + client.agents.register(loadAgentConfig(stubConfig)) + runServeCommand. It also injects dispatcherOptions.spawn (unsandboxed — bwrap userns nesting fails when the harness is itself run inside warren's sandbox) and a no-op dispatcherOptions.startProxy. Production warren still execs 'burrow serve' directly; this shim is harness-only.

Sample project burrow.toml in scripts/acceptance/lib/fixtures.ts declares [env].optional = ['WARREN_STUB_SLEEP_MS', 'WARREN_STUB_MULCH_DOMAIN', 'WARREN_STUB_MULCH_ID', 'WARREN_STUB_SEED_ID'] so the stub-agent.sh script can read knobs the harness sets via bootInProc.extraEnv. burrow's resolveEnv() drops keys missing from the host env, so scenarios that don't set them are unaffected; scenarios 05/06 use WARREN_STUB_SLEEP_MS=8000 to keep the agent alive across the warren-restart kill window. The script defaults are still defined in agent.sh so direct invocation outside the harness keeps working.

ScenarioCtx.lifecycle (assert.ts) is the optional process-control surface for scenarios that need killWarren/restartWarren/killBurrow (warren-647e scenario 06; future scenario 12 supervisor restart-budget). Populated in scripts/acceptance/run.ts from the BootHandle returned by bootInProc — undefined in container mode until the compose launcher exposes equivalent hooks. Scenarios that need it should declare modes:['in-proc'] and assert lifecycle !== undefined inside run() — calling without the guard is a hard error.

name: acceptance scenarios drive a long-running stub agent via [sleep_ms=N] in the prompt
description: Scenarios 07/08 (steer/cancel) need the warren run to stay non-terminal during the wire call, but burrow's declarative agent has no per-run env knob. The stub-agent.sh extracts a [sleep_ms=NNN] marker from its prompt arg via bash regex (\[sleep_ms=([0-9]+)\]) and sleeps that long before exiting; scenarios embed the marker in the user prompt to control agent lifetime. Used by 07-steer (sleep 8s) and 08-cancel (sleep 10s).

content: Acceptance scenarios that share a warren+burrow boot must lookup-then-create the sample project (GET /projects → POST /projects only when missing); a single warren+burrow pair persists across all scenarios in one harness invocation, and POST /projects with a duplicate gitUrl returns 400 validation_error. ensureSampleProject helpers in 07-steer.ts and 08-cancel.ts encode this.

Acceptance scenarios that count agents must filter to source !== 'builtin' before asserting size — the server boot-seeds claude-code + sapling built-ins (mx-f52e13), so an empty-list precondition is wrong. /agents responses carry source: 'builtin' | 'library' per row (handlers.ts withAgentSource); narrow on that field before counting. Fixed in scripts/acceptance/scenarios/02-agents-refresh.ts.

Acceptance stub agent (scripts/acceptance/lib/stub-agent/agent.sh) parses four prompt-driven knobs in addition to [sleep_ms=NNN]: [mulch_id=...], [mulch_ts=...], [seed_id=...], [seed_ts=...]. Lets reap-roundtrip scenarios drive deterministic LWW inputs without restarting warren or rewriting [env].optional in the project's burrow.toml. Mulch and seed timestamps default to the same wall-clock value but can diverge per-prompt.

description: Acceptance scenario 10 (reap seeds-close roundtrip) cannot test mode='updated' across two runs because .seeds/issues.jsonl is committed in the fixture project (fixtures.ts seeds ah-stub-1 then 'git add . && git commit').
resolution: Drop the second run; assert only mode='added' end-to-end. spawnRun's pre-spawn refreshProjectClone (mx-6e85b9: 'git checkout --force' + 'git reset --hard origin/<ref>') wipes reap's uncommitted writes to tracked files between spawns, so Run B's reap reads the original committed body and treats the incoming closed row as fresh. Mulch's acceptance.jsonl survives the refresh because only .mulch/.gitkeep is committed; that's why scenario 09 *can* exercise all three LWW branches across three sequential runs. mirrorClosedSeeds' updated branch is exercised in src/runs/reap.test.ts unit tests.

name: stub-cancel-reap
description: Acceptance scenarios that need to certify reap behavior on a stub-shell agent must drive cancel-on-running rather than waiting for natural termination. Stub-shell does not emit a 'state_change' result envelope (only claude-code does, per detectRuntimeTerminal in src/runs/stream.ts), so the bridge never auto-fires reap on natural exit. Pattern: spawn with [sleep_ms=4000], poll /runs/:id until state=running (warren-3c40 guard against queued→never_started misclassification), POST /runs/:id/cancel, poll for terminal. cancelRun reaps inline (mx-bade10) so reap.* events appear in the events log on the cancelled-not-succeeded path. Used by scenarios 09 and 10.

content: Bun.spawn auto-loads .env from cwd into spawned children even when env: {...} is explicitly set; child variables not in the explicit env still inherit from .env. Acceptance scenarios that spawn warren/supervisor/doctor children must explicitly set CANOPY_REPO_URL='', GITHUB_TOKEN='', BURROW_API_TOKEN='', WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN='' to neuter dev-only .env values that would otherwise corrupt fixture-based clones (CANOPY_REPO_URL=file:///canopy-source from .env hits canopy_clone false-negative; GITHUB_TOKEN triggers supervisor's git-credentials writer which clobbers GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL insteadOf rules).

name: supervisor-pid-file-via-burrow-shim
description: Acceptance scenario 12 discovers the burrow pid across supervisor restarts by pointing WARREN_BURROW_BIN at a tiny bash shim that does: 'shift; echo $$ > $WARREN_ACCEPTANCE_BURROW_PID_FILE; exec bun run $WARREN_ACCEPTANCE_BURROW_WRAPPER "$@"'. The shift drops the supervisor's 'serve' positional (burrow-with-stub.ts only knows --socket / --no-auth); after exec, $$ becomes the bun runtime's pid. Polling pidfile contents+mtime detects the supervisor's restart deterministically without lsof/fuser (macOS dev hosts ship neither). Production warren talks to plain 'burrow serve' — the shim is harness-only.

content: Acceptance scenarios that spawn 'warren doctor' as a child must override CANOPY_REPO_URL='' (per .env-clobber convention) and provide a fake bwrap shim on PATH because doctor's checkBwrap probes the literal 'bwrap --version' binary and macOS dev hosts don't ship bubblewrap. Shim layout: write '#!/usr/bin/env bash\nprintf bwrap 0.99.0-acceptance\n' to scratch/bin/bwrap, chmod +x, prepend dir to PATH. Scenarios needing canopy probes to pass instead must set CANOPY_REPO_URL to the fixture URL AND POST /agents/refresh first to clone the directory.

Acceptance ScenarioCtx exposes socketPath (in addition to warrenUrl, token, tmp, fixtures) so scenarios that need to point an out-of-band warren CLI (e.g. 'warren doctor' in scenario 11) at the running burrow socket don't have to derive '${tmp}/sock/burrow.sock' from the in-proc layout convention. Populated by run.ts from BootHandle.socketPath.

Scenario 13 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/13-container-smoke.ts) is the only container-only scenario — modes:['container']. Verifies the deploy shape that in-proc can't: image builds, supervisor boots burrow under apparmor/seccomp/systempaths-unconfined + cap_add=SYS_ADMIN, /healthz auth-exempt 200, GET /agents (with auth) returns the 2 builtins (claude-code, sapling) all source='builtin' since CANOPY_REPO_URL is blanked, /readyz returns structured {ok, checks: [...]} body. We do NOT assert ok=true on /readyz: bwrap probe behaviour on macOS Docker Desktop is host-dependent and canopy_clone reports 'no library configured' (info, not failure) when CANOPY_REPO_URL is unset (warren-d3e9). Lifecycle hooks are absent in container mode — the in-container supervisor owns burrow lifecycle, so killWarren/restartWarren/killBurrow stay in-proc-only.

Acceptance scenarios that mutate the shared sample-source repo (scripts/acceptance/lib/fixtures.ts) MUST clean up before AND after — the in-proc harness builds the fixture once per run, so .warren/ artifacts left from one scenario are visible to others. Scenario 14 (.warren/ lifecycle) shells 'git rm -r .warren/ && commit' on entry and again on exit; the entry-side cleanup is what makes the scenario re-runnable when --keep-tmp left state behind.

Scenario 14 (.warren/ lifecycle) tests doctor's warren_config check via /readyz, not by spawning 'warren doctor' — checkWarrenConfig is the same function in both surfaces (mx-718b25), and /readyz already runs in-process against the harness's project registry. Spawning doctor would require WARREN_DB_PATH override + a parallel projects-row install; /readyz reads deps.repos.projects.listAll() directly.

spawnRun's 'trigger' field accepts arbitrary strings — current call-sites: 'manual' (POST /runs default), 'cron' (scheduler dispatch), 'scheduled' (scheduled-for seed dispatch), 'manual-trigger' (POST /projects/:id/triggers/:triggerId/run Run Now). UI labels these in run history; keep new trigger labels kebab-case for consistency.

Acceptance scenario 15 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/15-triggers-roundtrip.ts) distinguishes the three trigger sources by the runs.trigger column: 'cron' (scheduler cron path), 'scheduled' (scheduledFor seeds), 'manual-trigger' (POST /projects/:id/triggers/:triggerId/run Run Now per warren-99c3). Per-source assertions are deltas against a pre-scenario baseline snapshot.

src/runs/seed.ts materializes pi_skills + pi_prompts canopy sections via JSONL '{name, body}' envelopes — one line per artifact. pi_skills writes to .pi/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (per-skill subdir); pi_prompts writes to .pi/prompts/<name>.md (flat). Both reject malformed JSON, non-object lines, missing/empty name, non-string body, duplicate names, and unsafe names (containing /, \\, '.' , or '..') with RunSpawnError — same shape as expertise_seed.

spawnRun accepts providerOverride/modelOverride strings (warren-f8c0). They are folded onto agent.frontmatter via withProviderOverrides BEFORE the row is created, so both runs.rendered_agent_json and the .canopy/agent.json seeded into the burrow workspace carry the override-applied frontmatter. POST /runs reads them from optionalString(body, 'providerOverride'|'modelOverride').

Acceptance scenario 16 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/16-pi-parity-smoke.ts) is the pi parity smoke. Because burrow 0.2.7 has no piRuntime (cross-repo step warren-0e06 not yet shipped), scripts/acceptance/lib/burrow-with-stub.ts registers a declarative 'pi' agent (loadAgentConfig pointing at tools/stub-agent.sh) alongside stub-shell. Scenario 16 asserts (a) GET /agents/pi returns source='builtin', (b) POST /runs agent='pi' returns 201 with burrowId/burrowRunId populated (proves burrow.up was called with agents:['pi']), (c) renderedAgentJson is frozen as the pi builtin, (d) at least one event lands in the events table within 15s. The 'agent_start' event-kind assertion is deferred to warren-70af (event-model widening) — for now any kind suffices because the declarative stub uses raw-text parser ('text' kind).

RunsRepo.attachStats (warren-a7dc) mirrors attachBurrow's partial-input semantics: omitted fields preserve, explicit null clears; throws ValidationError when called with no fields. Persists per-run cost_usd + tokens_input/output/cache_read/cache_write (migration 0006). All five columns are nullable and stay null for non-pi runtimes.

bridgeRunStream (warren-a7dc) accepts optional piStats: PiStatsClient. When set, the bridge snapshots get_session_stats on first observed event (baseline) and on agent_end OR terminalDetected (terminal), then persists (terminal - baseline) via attachStats. Failures are caught + logged via BridgeLogger.warn; the run never fails from a stats RPC error. Non-pi runs pass piStats=undefined and leave the cost columns null.

Cross-repo coordination seeds (e.g. warren-0e06 for pi runtime) gate the merge of their per-step worktree commits to main, NOT the per-step seed closures themselves. When child seeds (warren-d18e/846b/f8c0/a7dc/70af) close while the gate is still in_progress, their commits sit in .claude/worktrees/<branch>/ until the gate clears. Unstrand by cherry-picking each commit onto main in plan-step order (cherry-pick brings only the source-code diff; rebase surfaces stale .seeds/.mulch/SPEC/ROADMAP diffs that conflict needlessly because main has moved forward). Quality gates and seed-body refresh land on main with the merges.

spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts) forwards agent.frontmatter as Burrow run metadata via composeBurrowMetadata: dispatchRun receives { ...operatorMetadata, frontmatter: agent.frontmatter }. This is the warren-side half of the burrow piRuntime contract (burrow-b5b4 reads ctx.frontmatter off Run.metadataJson.frontmatter). The frontmatter is the post-override envelope (operator > .warren/defaults.json > builtin), so per-run provider/model overrides actually reach buildPiArgv. Operator-supplied 'frontmatter' keys in metadata are always overwritten by the agent's resolved envelope.

detectRuntimeTerminal (src/runs/stream.ts) matches two runtime terminal shapes on the shared kind=state_change/stream=system carrier: claude-code's payload.type='result' (with is_error mapping true→failed, else→succeeded) and pi's payload.type='agent_end' (always→succeeded; reap reconciles against burrow's exit_code if the process crashed afterwards). Adding a new runtime means extending this dispatch with the runtime's own terminal envelope — same carrier kind/stream, distinct payload.type. The bridge breaks on first match so any trailing events on the wire are silently dropped.

description: src/runs/stream.ts bridge poll-loop never breaks on pi runs: detectRuntimeTerminal only matched claude-code's payload.type='result', missing pi's payload.type='agent_end' lifecycle envelope. Symptom: burrow row finalizes (state=succeeded, exit_code=0, completed_at set) while warren row stays state=running, ended_at=null, cost_usd=null indefinitely — bridge keeps iterating burrow's infinite stream (the for-await loop at line 165), reap never fires, no PR opens. Restarting warren doesn't help because recoverActiveRunStreams re-enters the same code path. Reproduced live on warren main + burrow-cli 0.2.12.
resolution: Widen detectRuntimeTerminal to recognize payload.type==='agent_end' as 'succeeded' alongside the existing 'result' shape. The pi envelope carries no error flag — reap reconciles against burrow's authoritative exit_code if the run crashed after agent_end.

name: ss-recv-q diagnoses burrow→pi stdin pipe state
description: Diagnose burrow→pi stdio pipe state with 'ss -xnpe' filtered by the unix socket inodes found in /proc/<pi-pid>/fd/{0,1,2}. The Recv-Q / Send-Q columns on the pi-stdin pipe (pi side as reader, burrow side as writer) reveal whether bytes were written, flushed, or only buffered in userland. Pi sleeping in ep_poll with 1 libuv thread and Recv-Q=0 across all stdio means burrow never wrote the prompt; ~10 libuv threads with .pi/agent/auth.json present but no .pi/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl means bytes arrived but pi never finished parsing the command. This diagnostic cracked burrow-029d (missing flush) and burrow-faf5 (missing trailing newline) after weeks of hypothesizing.

name: pi RPC terminal envelope is agent_end (not result)
description: Pi's RPC-mode terminal envelope is {"type":"agent_end", ...} (state_change on stream=system), NOT {"type":"result", ...} which is claude-code's shape. Warren's detectRuntimeTerminal in src/runs/stream.ts has to recognize BOTH for the bridge loop to break and reap to fire — the runtime-specific terminal shape is the contract gate between the per-runtime burrow parser and warren's bridge.

content: Frontmatter wire from warren → burrow → piRuntime. spawnRun's composeBurrowMetadata (src/runs/spawn.ts) merges agent.frontmatter into the metadata POSTed on /runs; burrow stores it on Run.metadataJson; burrow's piRuntime reads it via ctx.frontmatter and substitutes provider/model into pi's argv via buildPiArgv. Warren-side resolution order is operator override > .warren/defaults.json > agent built-in frontmatter (composed in spawnRun via withProviderOverrides + readProjectDefaults). agent.frontmatter is also seeded onto /workspace/.canopy/agent.json for runtime introspection, but that file is informational — the metadata field on POST /runs is the actual wire.

src/runs/seed.ts is a pure builder (buildSeedFiles(agent: AgentDefinition): BuildSeedFilesResult) returning HttpWorkspaceFile[] with workspace-relative paths (.canopy/agent.json, .mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl, .seeds/workflow.txt, .pi/skills/<name>/SKILL.md, .pi/prompts/<name>.md). No fs/promises imports, no SeedFs seam. spawn.ts owns the disk write via writeSeedFilesToDisk (mkdir+writeFile, decodes base64). The seedWorkspace seam on SpawnRunInput receives {workspacePath, agent, files: HttpWorkspaceFile[]}; step 2 (warren-eaee) replaces writeSeedFilesToDisk with burrows.up({seed:{files}}) and removes the seam.

spawnRun threads buildSeedFiles output into burrowClient.http.burrows.up({ seed: { files } }) so provisioning + .canopy/.mulch/.seeds/.pi/ workspace drops land in a single atomic round-trip (R-07 / pl-a31c step 2); buildSeedFiles runs BEFORE the warren run row is created so expertise_seed/pi_skills validation failures throw cleanly with no half-spawned row to GC. The previous SeedWorkspaceInput / seedWorkspace injection seam and the temporary writeSeedFilesToDisk fs/promises adapter (step 1) are deleted; spawn.ts no longer imports node:fs/promises. Burrow rolls back its own side if a seed file is rejected, so a burrows.up failure surfaces as the caller's error verbatim — warren never observes a partially-provisioned burrow (burrow stays null in the catch, no DELETE fires).

src/runs/reap.ts mirrorClosedSeeds (warren-ae92) reads the workspace-side .seeds/issues.jsonl via input.burrowClient.http.files.read('.seeds/issues.jsonl') wrapped in withTransportMapping, NOT via fs.readFile on burrow.workspacePath. NotFoundError from @os-eco/burrow-cli is the no-op shape (file never created by the agent → return 0, no reap_failed); any other thrown error is rethrown so reapRun's outer catch records it as reap_failed step=seeds_close. The project-side .seeds/issues.jsonl (warren-tenant disk) still writes through ReapFs because reap is co-tenanted with the project clone. mulch_merge + branch_push remain disk-bound pending the burrow list-files endpoint follow-up (warren-7f7c).

reap.test.ts fakeBurrowClient (warren-ae92) accepts a second FakeBurrowClientOpts arg with two knobs: seedsIssuesBody (string → files.read returns { contents } for .seeds/issues.jsonl) and filesRead (full override). Default behaviour is to throw NotFoundError from files.read so every other test (which never seeds workspace-side seed content) silently exercises the 'agent never created the file' path. Both Burrow and NotFoundError import from '@os-eco/burrow-cli' on the same line.

Reap mulch_merge (src/runs/reap.ts mergeMulch) is the one warren↔burrow path still on shared-filesystem reads after warren-0a83 / pl-a31c. It does fs.readdir(.mulch/expertise/) to discover agent-controlled per-domain JSONL filenames, then fs.readFile each. Neither half maps onto R-07's current single-file HttpClient.files.read surface — the readdir step has no HTTP equivalent. Tracked downstream in burrow-18ca (workspace file listing endpoint: GET /burrows/:id/files with no path or ?prefix= for recursive listings, mirrors R-07's path-validation rules). When burrow-18ca ships, the warren-side migration is a mechanical swap: HttpClient.files.list({ prefix: '.mulch/expertise' }) -> HttpClient.files.read per entry; deletes the final fs/promises import from reap.ts and closes warren-0a83's seam violation completely.

name: spec-r07-seam-closure
description: SPEC §4.3 step 3 + step 6 and §11.A 'Seeding' / 'Reap' / 'Why HTTP-and-not-shared-disk' bullets pin the burrow-side warren↔burrow seam as HTTP (warren 0.2.0, pl-a31c). §11.A 'Seeding' bullet replaces the prior 'shell out to ml record via burrow exec (or equivalent)' sketch with: warren groups expertise_seed by domain, builds HttpWorkspaceFile[] entries, ships them as POST /burrows seed.files; burrow validates and writes atomically, rolling back on validation failure before warren observes a burrow_id. §11.A 'Reap' bullet pins seeds_close on HttpClient.files.read; mulch_merge stays disk-bound pending burrow-18ca. §12's mulch row clarifies host-side .mulch is still disk; per-run .mulch ships via seed.files. §5 ASCII diagram drops the stale 'ml record' shell-out — warren never shelled out to ml record (no call sites in src/).

content: spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts, warren-9993) composes the burrow workspace branch as `${prefix}/${run.id}` and passes it on burrows.up's branch field. Prefix resolution lives in src/runs/branch.ts (resolveRunBranchPrefix): project default (.warren/defaults.json.runBranchPrefix) > input.runBranchPrefixDefault (sourced from WARREN_RUN_BRANCH_PREFIX env by callers) > DEFAULT_RUN_BRANCH_PREFIX = 'burrow' (legacy default, kept for backward compat). Suffix is always the warren run_xxx id — burrow's default 'burrow/<bur-id>' is replaced so branches back-reference the warren row on git log / PR review. Pre-warren-9993 warren passed no branch and let burrow default; now warren always passes one.

bridgeRunStream's piStats branch (src/runs/stream.ts, warren-36c0) detects pi's terminal envelope via isPiAgentEnd(event) — kind='state_change' && stream='system' && payload.type==='agent_end' — mirroring burrow pi parser (src/runtime/parsers/pi.ts:86-98), not the synthetic kind==='agent_end' shape. Detection is parallel to detectRuntimeTerminal (warren-2687) which matches the same wire shape; both branches converge on the same envelope so persistPiStatsDelta fires once and the bridge breaks immediately after. Tests in src/runs/stream.test.ts use the piAgentEnd(burrowRunId, seq) helper to build the wire shape — never use synthetic kind:'agent_end' overrides.

PiStatsClient (src/runs/stream.ts:91) is defined and bridgeRunStream accepts input.piStats, but there is no production call-site that constructs one — grep -rn 'piStats' src/server/ returns no hits as of 2026-05-13. So cost/token columns on runs stay null on every pi run today regardless of bridge predicates; the warren-36c0 wire-shape fix is an unblock-enabler for whenever server/bridges.ts gets piStats wiring. Tracker: warren-17a4 captures the gated scenario-16 assertion.

bridgeRunStream (src/runs/stream.ts, warren-17a4) now does in-stream pi cost extraction by default — accumulatePiUsage reads payload.type=turn_end / message.usage.{input,output,cacheRead,cacheWrite,cost.total} on every event, and at the first agent_end (or terminalDetected) persistInStreamPiUsage calls RunsRepo.attachStats with the run-level totals. Pi v0.74 emits per-turn usage inline (see burrow src/runtime/parsers/__golden__/pi-v0.74.0-anthropic-*.jsonl), so no out-of-band RPC is needed. The PiStatsClient interface stays as an explicit override (sources warren can't observe in-stream); when both fire, the client wins for parity with the pre-warren-17a4 behavior tested in stream.test.ts.

placeForProject (src/runs/placement.ts, warren-135b / pl-9ba1 step 2) is a two-tier rule: (A) project-affinity — RunsRepo.mostRecentSucceededWithWorker(projectId) returns the worker of the newest succeeded run; affinity falls through if that worker is not in the healthy set (draining or unreachable workers cannot anchor affinity). (B) least-loaded — RunsRepo.countInflightByWorker counts queued+running rows grouped by workerId; ties break by alphabetical worker name. Draining + unreachable workers are filtered out before either tier. Throws NoEligibleWorkerError when no healthy workers exist.

placeForBurrow (src/runs/placement.ts, warren-135b) is sticky-by-burrow: reads burrows.workerId and returns it as long as the worker exists and is healthy OR draining (draining workers keep serving existing burrows until they finish; plan acceptance #3). Unreachable worker → StickyWorkerUnreachableError; missing worker row → StickyWorkerUnreachableError; missing burrow row → NoEligibleWorkerError. No silent re-placement on a dead worker (plan risk #5).

spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts, warren-39c3 / pl-9ba1 step 4) now takes `burrowClientPool: BurrowClientPool` instead of `burrowClient: BurrowClient`. Placement (`pool.placeFor({projectId})`) happens BEFORE `runs.create` so `runs.worker_id` lands at row creation; a `burrows` row capturing `{id, workerId}` is written between `attachBurrow.burrowId` and `attachBurrow.burrowRunId` so sticky-by-burrow (cancel/steer/reap/fan-out via `pool.clientFor`) has a durable mapping. The resolved `placement.client` services provision + dispatch + rollback so a single run never crosses workers.

Test wiring for spawnRun (post-warren-39c3): wrap a stubbed BurrowClient in a one-worker BurrowClientPool via `repos.workers.upsert({ name: 'local', url: 'unix:///tmp/x.sock' })` then `new BurrowClientPool({ repos }).register('local', client)`. The workers row gives placeForProject a healthy candidate; without it, placement raises NoEligibleWorkerError before any burrow call. Used as makePool/poolFor/fakeBurrowDeps helpers in src/runs/spawn.test.ts, src/server/scheduler.test.ts, src/server/handlers.test.ts, src/server/server.test.ts, src/cli/commands/run.test.ts.

cancelRun / steerRun / reapRun / bridgeRunStream (src/runs/, warren-c0c9 / pl-9ba1 step 5) all take `burrowClientPool: BurrowClientPool` instead of `burrowClient: BurrowClient`. Each resolves the owning worker via `pool.clientFor({ burrowId: run.burrowId })`. Propagates StickyWorkerUnreachableError (503 via src/server/errors.ts) when the pinned worker is unreachable. `bridgeRunStream` requires a new `burrowId: string` input field alongside `burrowRunId` so the run-stream poll lands on the same worker that owns the burrow.

name: Acceptance pi runtime: declarative spawn + parsePiEvents
description: To assert pi cost/token columns end-to-end without a real pi binary or burrow piRuntime (warren-0e06), scripts/acceptance/lib/burrow-with-stub.ts builds a custom AgentRuntime by calling loadAgentConfig() on a declarative pi config (outputFormat raw-text, command=tools/pi-stub-agent.sh) and spreading the result with parseEvents overridden to parsePiEvents imported from @os-eco/burrow-cli/src/runtime/parsers/pi.ts. The pi-stub-agent.sh fixture emits pi RPC JSONL (response/agent_start/turn_start/message_*/turn_end with message.usage.cost.total+tokens/agent_end). Bypasses burrow's outputFormat enum (raw-text|stream-json|jsonl-claude) without a cross-repo change. Use this when you need pi-shaped events flowing through warren's bridge in tests/acceptance.

content: Scenario 16 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/16-pi-parity-smoke.ts) asserts run.cost_usd / tokens_input / tokens_output are non-null+positive after the pi run completes by polling GET /runs/:id with a 15s deadline (waitForPiUsage). Backed by the pi stub emitting turn_end usage envelopes + agent_end; warren's bridge accumulatePiUsage+persistInStreamPiUsage (src/runs/stream.ts) populates the columns before the run terminalizes.

buildPrContent (src/runs/pr.ts, warren-9ee3) takes optional commits/diffStat/seed/startedAt/endedAt/cost+tokens fields and builds a structured body with sections: Summary, Run (link/agent/duration/cost), Seeds, Commits (N), Files changed, Prompt (collapsed <details>), footer. Title precedence: seed.title → first commit subject → first prompt line → 'warren run <id> (<agent>)' fallback. All extra fields are optional — buildPrContent degrades to the original prompt-line-as-title behavior when none are supplied. Phase 2 (LLM summary behind WARREN_PR_SUMMARY_AGENT) is deferred.

gatherPrContext (src/runs/reap.ts, warren-9ee3) runs three best-effort sub-calls in parallel before tryOpenPr: collectCommits ('git log --reverse --pretty=format:%H %s <base>..HEAD'), collectDiffStat ('git diff --stat <base>..HEAD'), resolveSeed (extract seed id via /\b([a-z][a-z-]*-[a-f0-9]{4,})\b/ from prompt, then 'sd show <id> --format json' in project.localPath). Every sub-call swallows errors and returns empty data — missing sd CLI, invalid base ref, or unparseable JSON all degrade to a less-rich PR body rather than failing the pr_open step.

RunsRepo.attachPreview (warren-e103) persists per-run preview env columns with attachStats-style partial-input semantics (mx-49272e): undefined skips, explicit null clears, ValidationError when no fields supplied. Five columns: previewState (enum 'starting'|'live'|'failed'|'torn-down'), previewPort (integer), previewStartedAt (ISO8601 TEXT), previewLastHitAt (ISO8601 TEXT, proxy-updated, debounced), previewFailureMessage (stderr tail TEXT). RunsRepo.create defaults all five to null on insert — non-opted-in projects (no .warren/defaults.json preview block) leave them untouched for the entire run lifetime. Async repo method, per pl-f17e step 1 async-first contract (mx-e6fad6).

name: PreviewPortAllocator: SQLite-backed allocator
description: src/preview/port-allocator.ts (warren-2277). Atomic allocate(runId, now?) inside drizzle db.transaction({ behavior: 'immediate' }) — BEGIN IMMEDIATE serializes writers so two concurrent allocators against the same db never claim the same port. Picks the lowest free port from a configurable range by SELECTing preview_port FROM runs WHERE preview_state IN ('starting','live') AND preview_port IS NOT NULL, then UPDATE runs SET preview_port + preview_state='starting' + preview_started_at WHERE id=:runId. Idempotent: a run already in starting/live returns its existing port unchanged. Exhaustion returns { status: 'exhausted' } (caller emits preview_failed event with reason='port_exhausted'); range exhaustion does NOT mutate the candidate row, so a retry after eviction is clean. Default range 30000-31000 via DEFAULT_PREVIEW_PORT_RANGE; env override via WARREN_PREVIEW_PORT_RANGE='<start>-<end>'. usage() snapshots {inUse,total,range} for the doctor/readyz preview_port_allocator probe (PREVIEW_PORT_USAGE_WARN_RATIO=0.8 → ok=false at ≥80% saturation).

content: Preview port restart-safety has no extra state — the runs table is the only source-of-truth. A fresh PreviewPortAllocator on the same db sees in-use ports via SELECT preview_port FROM runs WHERE preview_state IN ('starting','live'). Release means another path (eviction worker, manual teardown) sets preview_state to 'torn-down' or 'failed'; the next SELECT naturally omits the port via the state filter, so even a stale preview_port left behind on a 'failed' row is correctly treated as free. Tested via the 'fresh allocator sees committed in-use ports' case in src/preview/port-allocator.test.ts.

name: reap-preview-launch-substep
description: Reap's 5th best-effort sub-step launches a per-run preview environment when outcome=succeeded + project opted in (.warren/defaults.json preview block) + worker=local. Mirrors pr_open (mx-05abb2): only fires on succeeded, never fails the run, emits reap_failed events on error with step='preview_launch'. Wires through src/preview/launch.ts (launchPreview): allocates a port via PreviewPortAllocator, spawns 'sh -c <preview.command>' as a burrow sidecar with inboundPortForward={hostPort,sandboxPort=preview.port}, then polls http://127.0.0.1:<hostPort><readiness_path or '/'> until 2xx (3xx also treated as ready for dev-server redirects). On 2xx writes preview_state=live + previewLastHitAt; on timeout writes preview_state=failed with stderr-tail in preview_failure_message + clears preview_port + safe-deletes the sidecar so the allocator recycles. Non-local worker skips with a reap_failed referencing R-12. Result envelope: { previewState: 'live'|'failed'|null, previewPort, previewUrl }.

name: reap-pr-annotate-preview-substep
description: Reap's 6th best-effort sub-step (pr_annotate_preview) idempotently patches the PR body's preview_url_or_placeholder fragment with either the live preview URL or the launch failure tail. Markers: <!-- warren:preview-start --> and <!-- warren:preview-end -->. Gated on pr_open producing a prUrl AND preview_launch reaching live|failed AND WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST set for the live case (failure tail can patch even without host). Mechanics in src/runs/pr-annotate.ts: parses owner/repo/N from the PR url (web or REST API shape), GETs current body, replaces (or appends ## Preview section if markers absent), PATCHes /repos/.../pulls/:n. Idempotent on re-run because markers are always exactly once. URL format: https://run-<id>.<WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST>.

content: buildPrContent (src/runs/pr.ts) takes optional previewOptedIn:boolean (warren-f156). When true, the PR body includes a ## Preview section bracketed by <!-- warren:preview-start --> and <!-- warren:preview-end --> with placeholder text 'Preview launching…'. pr_annotate_preview replaces the content between the markers post-launch. Constants PREVIEW_FRAGMENT_START / PREVIEW_FRAGMENT_END exported so the annotator matches the literals without drift.

title: preview launch reads sidecar logs for failure tail, not stdin/stdout streams
rationale: Burrow's sidecar registry exposes GET /burrows/:id/sidecars/:scid/logs as the only post-mortem surface — process streams are consumed by burrow's ring-buffer. For warren-f156's readiness_timeout / sidecar_exited failure paths we call sidecars.logs({tailBytes: 4096}) and copy stderr (falling back to stdout if stderr is empty) into runs.preview_failure_message. Keeps reap's failure surface attributable to the agent's output (SPEC §11.L 'reviewers read agent broke the build').

name: preview-eviction-worker
description: Preview eviction worker (src/preview/eviction.ts, warren-ea6b) is an in-process tick loop wired into bootServer alongside the scheduler/worker-probe (mirrors src/triggers/tick.ts startScheduler shape). Combines four signals from SPEC §11.L: (1) idle TTL primary — now-preview_last_hit_at>idle_ttl (falls back to preview_started_at when last_hit_at is null so launched-but-never-visited previews still age out), (2) max_lifetime ceiling — now-preview_started_at>max_lifetime, (3) global LRU cap when survivor count > WARREN_PREVIEW_MAX_LIVE (default 20) — sorts by idleSince asc, evicts the longest-idle. Per-project .warren/defaults.json preview.idle_ttl / preview.max_lifetime override the global env. Each eviction: BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction CAS that bails when state moved (manual-teardown re-entrant per design lock), best-effort sidecars.list+delete via pool resolver, preview_state→torn-down + preview_port→null, emit preview_evicted{reason∈idle_ttl|max_lifetime|lru, port, previousState}. Single-flight tick like the scheduler; resolveSidecar + previews repo injected for tests.

content: parseDurationMs (src/preview/duration.ts, warren-ea6b) converts schema-validated duration strings (30m, 1h30m, 500ms) to ms; thin shape-recheck on top of DurationStringSchema (mx-f7cf24). Consumers: preview eviction worker reads idle_ttl/max_lifetime from .warren/defaults.json preview block.

Preview proxy debounce (src/preview/proxy.ts, warren-8a10) writes runs.preview_last_hit_at BEFORE forwarding to the upstream (SPEC §11.L: a slow upstream response must not make a live preview look idle to the eviction worker). Debounced to ~once per 30s per runId via a process-local Map; warren restart forgets the map but the persisted column is the source of truth so eviction never false-triggers.

name: preview-manual-teardown-route
description: Manual preview teardown route (POST /runs/:id/preview/teardown, warren-d725) is an idempotent operator counterpart to the eviction worker. Lives in src/preview/teardown.ts as teardownPreview(); the handler in src/server/handlers.ts narrows deps.db on dialect (503s on pg / missing-db) and constructs createRunPreviewsRepo(deps.db) inline. CAS is RunPreviewsRepo.claimTeardown — BEGIN IMMEDIATE serializes against the eviction worker's evict, returns {status:'torn-down'|'already-torn-down'|'already-failed'|'never-launched', previousState, port, burrowId}. Only 'torn-down' fires the sidecar.list+delete best-effort and emits the preview_torn_down audit event; the other three branches are no-op idempotent (mirrors cancelRun's alreadyTerminal flag). Event payload: {actor, port, previousState} — actor defaults to 'manual' from the optional {actor} body field. Route is bearer-gated (not in isAuthExempt). Returns 200 for every CAS outcome; 404 for unknown runId; 503 when db is undefined or pg-dialect.

title: Preview runs in the agent's sandbox, not a fork
rationale: SPEC §11.L (R-19) explicitly chose same-sandbox over forking the workspace from the agent's final commit. Forking doubles burrow workload + complicates port binding for marginal safety; the agent's side effects (deleted files, half-applied migrations, busy ports) are accepted as the cost of realness. Failure surfaces as preview_state='failed' with the stderr tail in preview_failure_message — reviewers read 'agent broke the build', attributable to the agent's output, not warren. Forked-sandbox can be a V2-of-this-item flag if a real operator hits a security incident.

title: Cross-host preview routing deferred to R-12 — proxy preamble returns 501 with explicit R-12 message
rationale: Remote workers (R-12) are still in design (burrow-c47a). Rather than guess at a worker-aware routing layer for previews and force a rework when R-12 lands, SPEC §11.L (R-19) explicitly deferred cross-host preview routing to a V2-of-this-item. The proxy preamble in src/preview/proxy.ts asserts local-worker-only via runs.worker_id and returns 501 with an explicit R-12 deferral message on mismatch. Silent fall-through to a closed loopback port would manifest as 'preview works for some runs, not others' — naming the deferral in the response makes the surface honest. Acceptance scenario 20 asserts the 501 path. When R-12 lands, the proxy preamble is the natural place to splice the worker-aware routing layer in.

title: Preview eviction: idle-TTL primary, max-lifetime ceiling, LRU cap, manual teardown — not wall-clock-TTL-only
rationale: ROADMAP R-19's first sketch used a single 'preview.ttl' wall-clock value. SPEC §11.L rejected this — a reviewer mid-session shouldn't have their preview yanked because N hours elapsed since launch. Four signals combine instead: (1) Idle TTL (default 30m, WARREN_PREVIEW_IDLE_TTL) — primary kill-rule, measured from proxy-tracked runs.preview_last_hit_at (debounced ~once per 30s per run; fallback to preview_started_at for launched-but-never-visited previews so they still age out). (2) Max-lifetime ceiling (default 8h, WARREN_PREVIEW_MAX_LIFETIME) — hard cap regardless of activity, kills 'browser tab from yesterday' failure mode. (3) Global LRU cap (default 20, WARREN_PREVIEW_MAX_LIVE) — bounds container memory; when reached the longest-idle preview evicts. (4) Manual teardown (POST /runs/:id/preview/teardown) — idempotent operator recourse. Per-project overrides for idle_ttl / max_lifetime live in .warren/defaults.json. All four eviction paths free the port via the same CAS and stop the sidecar; the burrow workspace stays (cleanup keyed off run lifecycle, not preview's). Defaults tuned for a home server — operators tune up for org-scale.

content: buildPrContent (src/runs/pr.ts, warren-bd49) composes the PR body from a fixed fragment registry in src/runs/pr-template.ts — PR_FRAGMENT_NAMES = title, summary, run, seeds, preview_url_or_placeholder, commits, files_changed, prompt, trailer. composeBody walks PR_BODY_FRAGMENT_NAMES (title excluded) in order; project overrides via .warren/pr-template.md replace fragments by name. Title precedence: project title override > seed.title > first commit subject > first prompt line > 'warren run <id> (<agent>)' fallback. The R-19 preview_url_or_placeholder is one entry in the registry, not a special case.

parsePrTemplate (src/runs/pr-template.ts) normalizes fragment names to snake_case: trim().toLowerCase().replace(/[\s-]+/g, '_'). So '## Files Changed', '## files-changed', and '## files_changed' all resolve to fragment 'files_changed'. Bodies have leading + trailing blank lines stripped; whitespace-only body = remove the fragment from output (matches canopy section-merge empty-body semantics).

title: PR-template V1 ships static-text overrides, no variable interpolation
rationale: warren-bd49 acceptance only requires that buildPrContent consumes named fragments (refactor) and that .warren/pr-template.md can override individual fragments. Supporting {{var}} substitution would force a variables map (run_id, agent, prompt, cost, commits...), escaping rules, and test surface for unknown placeholders. V1 keeps it simple: an override is literal markdown. Projects that need per-run dynamic data keep the default fragment for that section. Deferring interpolation lets a future seed add it without locking syntax now.

name: preview-allocator-pg-advisory-lock
description: PreviewPortAllocator (src/preview/port-allocator.ts, warren-adfb) serializes allocations across pg processes via 'SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock($1)' inside DrizzleAdapter.runInTransaction (lock auto-released on commit). Within a single warren process, a per-instance Promise-chain mutex (allocateChain) serializes allocate() calls — necessary because sqlite's single-connection runInTransaction would otherwise BEGIN-inside-BEGIN under Promise.all, AND because two parallel snapshots could otherwise pick the same free port. The mutex stores .catch(()=>undefined) so a failing allocation doesn't poison the chain.

content: createRunPreviewsRepo (src/preview/eviction.ts, warren-adfb) is dialect-polymorphic — takes AnyWarrenDb and wraps it in DrizzleAdapter.for(db). evict() is a single-statement CAS via UPDATE...WHERE id=X AND state IN ('starting','live') RETURNING {id} (atomic on both dialects, no tx needed; second writer sees zero RETURNING rows and reports claimed=false). claimTeardown does SELECT-then-UPDATE inside runInTransaction; pg branch grabs 'SELECT id FROM runs WHERE id = $1 FOR UPDATE' (hand-rolled SQL via pgDb.execute(sql`...`) because .for('update') is pg-only and txRuns is sqlite-typed via the schema getter). The UPDATE keeps a state IN ('starting','live') filter as belt-and-suspenders CAS.

name: preview-proxy-mode-dispatch
description: Preview proxy preamble (src/preview/proxy.ts, warren-8085) uses a discriminated-union config on `mode`: `{mode:'subdomain', host:string}` or `{mode:'path'}`. The handler picks the runId extractor by mode — parseRunIdFromHost on `Host: run-<id>.<host>` for subdomain, parsePreviewPathPrefix on `/p/<runId>(/<rest>)?` for path — then shares one inner pipeline (run lookup, R-12 501, 503 not-live, 426 WS, 401 cookie, debounced last_hit_at, 502 unreachable). In path mode url.search is preserved separately from rest (parsePreviewPathPrefix takes a pathname, not a full URL); upstream URL is http://127.0.0.1:<port><rest><search>. previewUnauthorized's 401 hint is mode-aware: subdomain mode emits an absolute URL keyed off config.host, path mode emits a URL derived from the request's url.origin. RunId charset for path matching is [A-Za-z0-9_-]+ — single-segment-only protects against path-traversal escapes; the DB lookup is the source of truth for whether the runId is real.

content: Path-mode HTML rewriting in src/preview/proxy.ts (warren-ab3a) layers two best-effort transforms onto the upstream Response, gated by mode='path' (the call site passes pathPrefix='/p/<runId>' or null): (1) injectBaseHref does byte-level splicing of '<base href="<pathPrefix>/">' after the opening <head> tag when content-type matches isHtmlContentType (text/html with optional ;params); idempotent — skipped when '<base' followed by space/tab/CR/LF/'/'/'>' already appears in the lookahead window so re-proxying warren-served HTML is a no-op. Bounded to HTML_HEAD_LOOKAHEAD_BYTES (64 KiB); documents with <head> past that window stream through unchanged. (2) rewriteLocationHeader prefixes a same-origin absolute path Location header (starts with single '/', not '//'); leaves absolute URLs, scheme-relative URLs, and values already under '<pathPrefix>/' untouched. Both transforms skip when content-encoding is set to anything other than identity (avoid smuggling plaintext under a gzip label). Subdomain mode passes Response through verbatim — the seam is mode-aware in applyPathModeRewrites only.

createPreviewAuth scope discriminator (src/preview/cookie.ts, warren-edff): {mode:'subdomain', cookieDomain} emits Path=/; Domain=<value>; {mode:'path', pathPrefix?} emits Path=<prefix>/<runId>/ (default /p) with NO Domain attribute. verifyCookie is mode-agnostic — browser-enforced Path scope is what isolates sibling-run sessions on the same warren host (SPEC §11.L risk 4). Replaces the previous cookieDomain top-level option.

formatPreviewUrl (src/preview/launch.ts, warren-c3c4) is mode-aware: subdomain → 'https://run-<id>.<host>' (no trailing slash, preserved from original §11.L), path → 'https://<host>/p/<id>/' (trailing slash is load-bearing so root-relative HTML resolves under the prefix). Reap's pr_annotate_preview branch (src/runs/reap.ts) passes input.previewLaunchConfig.mode (default DEFAULT_PREVIEW_MODE='path'); WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST must still be set in path mode for annotation (proxy can derive origin from inbound Host, but PR comments need an absolute URL).

extractClaudeUsage (src/runs/stream.ts, warren-87f9) shape-sniffs claude-code's terminal result envelope (type='result', total_cost_usd + usage.{input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens}) and assigns (not accumulates) into a SessionStatsAccumulator — single-shot because claude-code emits cumulative run totals exactly once. Sibling to accumulatePiUsage. Persisted via the renamed persistInStreamUsage (was persistInStreamPiUsage) which now carries a runtime: 'pi' | 'claude' log tag. Terminal dispatch order: piStats override > piUsage.seen > claudeUsage.seen — pi wins when both shapes appear in one stream (mixed-runtime parity).

name: preview-failure-tail-location
description: Per-run preview failure tail (stderr/stdout of crashed sidecar) is captured by captureFailureTail in src/preview/launch.ts:229 — pulls last 4KB via sidecars.logs(burrowId, sidecarId, {tailBytes: PREVIEW_FAILURE_TAIL_BYTES=4096}), prefers stderr, falls back to stdout. composeFailureMessage joins the readiness headline + '\n\n' + tail; result stored on runs.preview_failure_message via attachPreview. The fly log line for the reap step only carries the headline (e.g. 'readiness_timeout: ...'); the full tail is only retrievable from the row. UI surfaces it at src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx:384-391 (PreviewCard renders previewFailureMessage in a <pre> when state==='failed'). API: GET /runs/<id> returns previewFailureMessage in the JSON payload. When diagnosing a previewState:'failed' run, don't stop at fly logs — pull the API row or open RunDetail to see why the sidecar crashed.

content: RunsRepo.listAll/listByProject/listByAgent (src/db/repos/runs.ts) take an options bag { limit?, sort?: 'started'|'cost', dir?: 'asc'|'desc' } as of warren-fd4b. sort='cost' uses raw sql NULLS LAST in both directions so unbilled runs sink; asc(runs.id) stays as the stable tiebreaker. GET /runs accepts ?sort=&dir= validated via parseRunsSort in src/server/handlers.ts. All in-tree callers passed no positional limit, so the API change is source-compatible.

spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts, pl-bb70 step 3 / warren-805a) accepts optional seedId:string on SpawnRunInput and forwards it onto repos.runs.create as the new seedId field. POST /runs (src/server/handlers.ts createRunHandler) pulls seedId via optionalString and spreads it on when defined. Persisted as runs.seed_id (nullable text in both sqlite + postgres). Sets up pl-bb70 step 4 — updateExtensions(seedId, {role, trigger, lastRunId, lastRunAt}) merge fires after dispatch — by giving the post-dispatch path the seed binding to write back to. Manual prompts and legacy runs leave it null.

spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts, pl-bb70 step 4 / warren-46cd) writes warren-namespaced seeds.extensions after dispatch when both `seedId` and `seedsCli: SeedsCliDeps` are wired. Payload is {role: agent.name, trigger: WarrenTriggerKind.safeParse(input.trigger ?? 'manual'), lastRunId: run.id, lastRunAt: nowIso} — trigger strings that don't parse against the strict enum (e.g. handlers.ts:354 'manual-trigger') are dropped so the other fields still merge instead of failing the whole write. Failures shell-out non-zero → seeds_extension_write_failed system event appended on the run (stream='system', burrowEventSeq=maxSeq+1, payload={seedId, reason}) and the dispatch is NOT rolled back, mirroring the pl-2f15 risk-4 mitigation in src/triggers/tick.ts recordClearFailure. Wired through ServerDeps.seedsCli (built in server/main.ts from {sdBinary: schedulerConfig.sdBinary, spawn: defaultSpawn}) into createRunHandler + runProjectTriggerHandler + bootScheduler's spawnDispatch.

R-01 producer side (pl-bb70, 2026-05-15) shipped: src/seeds-cli/ owns the warren-namespaced extensions surface, and every successful dispatch in warren (spawnRun + cron tick) writes back to the originating seed via a single updateExtensions(deps.seedsCli, projectPath, seedId, payload) call. Payload shape is WarrenExtensionsSchema.strict() with optional {role, trigger, lastRunId, lastRunAt, scheduledFor, lastScheduledRun}; cron tick collapses today's clear + post-dispatch stamp into one merged write {scheduledFor:null, lastScheduledRun:runId, role, trigger:'cron', lastRunId, lastRunAt}. The runs row has a nullable seed_id column threaded from POST /runs.seedId through spawnRun (mx-d85ad7) so warren stores the inverse pointer; RunDetail surfaces a back-link when seedId is present. Failure mode (mx-41ed65): updateExtensions errors append a seeds_extension_write_failed system event on the run and do NOT roll back the dispatch, mirroring the pl-2f15 risk-4 mitigation in triggers/tick.ts. Adding a new dispatch source (webhook, comment, manual-trigger) is now a three-line change: thread seedId through SpawnRunInput, pick a WarrenTriggerKind enum value, let spawnRun's existing post-dispatch path do the write — no per-call-site shell-out logic.

spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts, R-03 / pl-fef5 step 7 / warren-0a7e) resolves the agent row via repos.agents.resolve(name, { projectId: input.projectId }) — project-tier first, global (built-in + library) tier as fallback. A null result re-raises as NotFoundError with the same recoveryHint shape ('POST /agents/refresh to re-discover from canopy') that the old repos.agents.require() used, so HTTP/CLI error envelopes are unchanged. The chosen tier's frontmatter (incl. source: 'project:<id>' for project-tier rows) is what readCachedAgent reads and what eventually lands on runs.rendered_agent_json after withProviderOverrides.

reap.ts (warren-0928) parses input.previewConfig.readiness_timeout via parseDurationMs and passes it as readinessTimeoutMs into launchPreview; absent → undefined → launcher falls through to DEFAULT_READINESS_TIMEOUT_MS (5m). The duration string was bounds-checked at load time by PreviewReadinessTimeoutSchema, so parseDurationMs is infallible at the call site.

name: preview-probe-per-call-timeout
description: probeOnce in src/preview/launch.ts (warren-33eb) wraps each fetch in an AbortController with PROBE_PER_CALL_TIMEOUT_MS (default 2_000ms, injectable via LaunchPreviewInput.probePerCallTimeoutMs for tests). Without this, a fetch that accepted TCP but stalled waiting for response bytes (e.g. burrow forwarder up but Next.js mid-compile) blocked indefinitely — the outer readinessTimeoutMs deadline check only runs between probe returns. The signal is cleared in a finally{} so the AbortController is GC'd whether the response was 2xx, non-2xx, or threw. run_7jjpt2jn9ej5 (jayminwest.com deployment) sat in starting for 6m47s with a 5m deadline before this fix.

content: launchPreview (src/preview/launch.ts, warren-79b2) injects a default env block {HOST: '0.0.0.0', HOSTNAME: '0.0.0.0', PORT: String(previewConfig.port)} into every preview sidecar via PreviewSidecarsClient.create.env. Fixes the bug class where dev servers binding to localhost/::1 only (Next.js 13.5+ default) are unreachable from burrow's inbound forwarder (which connects via nc 127.0.0.1 inside the netns). CRA / Express-style servers honor HOST/HOSTNAME automatically. Vite/Next.js/Express read PORT so operators don't duplicate the sandbox port. Project commands override by inlining HOST=... / PORT=... ahead of the command (sh -c semantics). Caveat documented in .warren/preview.yaml stub: Next.js's CLI silently IGNORES HOSTNAME/HOST env vars (commander -H/--hostname is NOT chained with .env(...)); Next.js projects must still pass -H 0.0.0.0 in their command:.

launchPreview (src/preview/launch.ts, warren-d9e7) runs a separate setup sidecar before the dev-server sidecar when previewConfig.setup is set. The setup sidecar is spawned without inboundPortForward/env (workspace cwd inherits); the launcher polls sidecars.get() every DEFAULT_SETUP_POLL_MS (1s) until state==='exited' or the wall-clock setupTimeoutMs (default DEFAULT_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS=300_000) is hit. Non-zero exit → reason='setup_failed' with stderr tail in preview_failure_message; never-exit → reason='setup_timeout'. On success the setup sidecar is best-effort deleted, then the dev-server sidecar spawns as before. Two new LaunchFailureReason variants: setup_failed | setup_timeout.

Path-mode preview cookie (src/preview/cookie.ts, warren-63e1) is now named warren_preview_<runId> with Path=/ — was warren_preview with Path=/p/<runId>/. SignedCookie.name is mode-aware: subdomain mode keeps the bare 'warren_preview' name with Path=/; path mode emits 'warren_preview_<runId>'. The per-run literal suffix preserves the SPEC §11.L risk-4 isolation between sibling preview sessions while Path=/ ensures the browser ships the cookie on every same-origin request — that's what makes referer-based asset routing in src/preview/proxy.ts able to authenticate '/_next/static/...'-style asset loads. previewCookieName(runId, mode) is the exported helper.

name: preview-proxy-referer-routing
description: In path mode, src/preview/proxy.ts (warren-63e1) consults Referer to route asset sub-resource requests. When url.pathname doesn't match /p/<id>/..., the preamble parses Referer's pathname for /p/<id>/...; on a hit (and when isWarrenApiPath(url.pathname) is false), it forwards url.pathname verbatim to the matched run's upstream port. parseRunIdFromReferer(headerValue) is the entry point. isWarrenApiPath duplicates handlers.API_PREFIXES locally with a parity test (warren-api-prefixes-stay-in-sync) so a new API surface in handlers.ts surfaces as a failed assertion.

content: rewriteRootRelativeAttrs (src/preview/proxy.ts, warren-63e1) is the third path-mode HTML transform after injectBaseHref + rewriteLocationHeader. It byte-scans href/src/srcset attribute values within the HTML_HEAD_LOOKAHEAD_BYTES (64 KiB) window and prefixes root-relative paths with /p/<runId>. Skips protocol-relative (//host), absolute URLs (http/https), data:, #, ?, and values already under <pathPrefix>/. srcset is split on commas with per-entry rewrite preserving inter-entry whitespace. Attribute boundary check (prev byte must be whitespace/</) rejects substring matches like data-src=.

spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts, warren-a8c3) gates plot_id on project.hasPlot before creating the warren row — a non-empty plot_id against a project whose clone has no .plot/ raises a typed ValidationError with a recoveryHint pointing at plot init + refresh. Empty / undefined plot_id is treated identically (no gate, no write).

spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts, warren-e26f / pl-2047 step 4) injects per-run env vars onto the burrow up call when the run is bound to a Plot. composePlotEnv returns {PLOT_ID: <run.plotId>, PLOT_ACTOR: 'agent:<agent-name>:<run-id>'} when run.plotId is non-null; returns null otherwise so non-Plot dispatches send the same body byte-for-byte. plotId is already validated against project.hasPlot earlier (warren-a8c3), so this site doesn't re-check. Routed via client.burrowsUp (not client.http.burrows.up) because burrow-cli@0.3.1 HttpBurrowsClient.up allowlists known fields and silently drops the rest — env wouldn't reach the wire through it. Asserted in spawn.test.ts.

spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts, warren-e848 / pl-2047 step 5) emits a run_dispatched event to the originating Plot AFTER attachBurrow(burrowRunId) succeeds, gated on updated.plotId !== null. Fire-and-log posture identical to writeSeedExtensions: failure records a 'plot_run_dispatched_failed' system event on the run via repos.events.append with payload {plotId, reason} and DOES NOT roll the dispatch back.
