The append is dispatched through a SpawnPlotAppender seam (SpawnRunInput.plotAppender) so tests stub without touching disk; default opens a UserPlotClient against join(projectAfterRefresh.localPath, '.plot'), calls handle.append({type:'run_dispatched', data:{run_id, agent, model, project}}), and on first-attempt failure best-effort client.rebuildIndex() then retries once (covers the seed's documented 'missing .plot/.index.db on first dispatch' path). model is extracted from agent.frontmatter.model (string|undefined → string|null in the payload).

title: Default dispatcher handle 'operator' instead of plumbing user-auth surface
rationale: warren-e848 needs a user:<handle> actor for the Plot run_dispatched event, but warren has no first-class user-auth surface today (acknowledged as pl-2047 risk #4 — cron/webhook dispatches also lack a user actor). Wiring real user auth across the request path is out of scope for this seed; instead spawnRun accepts an optional dispatcherHandle (POST /runs body field, forwarded verbatim) and DEFAULT_DISPATCHER_HANDLE='operator' is the fallback when undefined/empty/malformed. resolveDispatcherHandle validates against the actor segment regex [A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9_-]* (copied from @os-eco/plot-cli/actor.ts) and downgrades malformed input rather than throwing — the append is fire-and-log, and a bad handle shouldn't be the reason a Plot record is missing. Future user-identity work can replace the fallback without changing the spawn contract.

content: mergePlot (src/runs/reap.ts, warren-7e0f / pl-2047 step 6) is reap's 5th best-effort sub-step (between seeds_close and branch_push). Per-plot-id replay of the burrow workspace .plot/pl-*.events.jsonl and pl-*.json into the project's persistent .plot/. Events are append-only and dedup by exact-line content (idempotent re-runs append 0); pl-*.json is LWW on updated_at with equal-ts+content-drift emitting plot.conflict. Agent-emitted decision_made / question_posed / artifact_produced events in the appended tail mirror into warren's event stream as plot.{type} kinds tagged with plotId. ReapStep type gained 'plot_merge'; ReapRunResult gained plotEventsAppended, plotsUpdated, plotEventsMirrored. Failure emits reap_failed step=plot_merge and never fails the run.

name: plot-client-write-acl-tests
description: src/plot-client/handle.test.ts + client.test.ts (warren-8b13 / pl-2047 step 7) bracket the SPEC §6 write-ACL narrowing two ways: runtime via expect(call).toThrow(PlotAgentACLViolationError) for AgentPlotHandle.append on each HUMANS_ONLY_EVENT_TYPES entry (intent_edited / status_changed / attachment_removed / question_answered) — the facade throws synchronously before returning the Promise, so .toThrow is required (.rejects misses it); compile-time via @ts-expect-error pinning that AgentPlotHandle has no editIntent/setStatus/detach methods and that append({type: "intent_edited" | "status_changed" | "attachment_removed" | "question_answered"}) is a TS error. test.each on HUMANS_ONLY_EVENT_TYPES needs HUMANS_ONLY_EVENT_TYPES.map((t) => [t] as const) because bun:test's table param expects rows, not bare scalars.

name: plot-id-prefix-rename-reap-prod-touch
description: Plot ID prefix rename pl- → plot- (warren-20f3, 2026-05-17): when plot-cli ships a breaking ID prefix change, warren needs to update both test fixtures AND production code that filters .plot/<prefix>-*.{json,events.jsonl} filenames. src/runs/reap.ts mergePlotsFromBurrow loops over name.startsWith('plot-') for events.jsonl and json files — change in lockstep with the plot-cli version pin (package.json + bun.lock) and run reap.test.ts to catch the production-code touchpoint the rename ticket missed enumerating.

content: checkPullRequestMerged (src/runs/pr.ts, warren-9e4c) is the pure GET /repos/:owner/:repo/pulls/:number poll helper for the PlanRun coordinator (warren-2623). Auth posture mirrors openPullRequest: GITHUB_TOKEN via 'Bearer <token>', injected fetch seam, Accept: application/vnd.github+json. Returns a 5-shape discriminated union {kind:'merged',mergedAt} | {kind:'open'} | {kind:'closed_unmerged'} | {kind:'missing_token',message} | {kind:'http_error',status,message}; status 0 on fetch-throw. Pairs with parsePullRequestUrl which only matches https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/<n> — returns null for GHE-hosted shapes so the coordinator treats them as 'cannot verify merge'. Pure helper: caller (coordinator) decides open=wait, closed_unmerged=plan_failed reason 'pr_closed_without_merge'.

name: WARREN_GH_FETCH_OVERRIDE acceptance seam
description: src/runs/pr.ts: openPullRequest + checkPullRequestMerged short-circuit to canned positive responses when process.env.WARREN_GH_FETCH_OVERRIDE === 'merged' (returns synthetic https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/1 URL + {kind:'merged', mergedAt:<now>}). loadAutoOpenPrConfigFromEnv synthesizes token='stub-token' when the override is set so reap's empty-token gate (input.autoOpenPr.token !== '') and tryOpenPr's token === '' gate don't skip pr_open. Acceptance-scoped (scenario 26); unset in production. The seam lives in pr.ts; pr-merge.ts wraps checkPullRequestMerged so the override is picked up transitively.

name: best-effort-plot-edge-with-warren-state-untouched
description: Plot-edge calls from warren handlers/coordinators are fire-and-log: a Plot-write failure logs a system event (plot_*_failed) and returns, never rolling back persisted warren state. Pattern shape applied at three seams in pl-7937 (PlanRun + Plot composition): emitPlanRunDispatchedToPlot (handler, post-persist; plan_run.plot_append_failed), per-child defaultPlotAppender (spawn, pre-burrow-up; plot_run_dispatched_failed system event from §11.O), and autoTransitionPlotToDone (coordinator, plan_succeeded branch; plan_run.plot_status_skipped / plan_run.plot_auto_done_failed). Each seam guards with a Plot-side precondition (active status, etc.) before writing, catches everything around the write, and surfaces the outcome as a discriminated AutoTransitionResult / typed log line so callers can emit observability events without coupling to Plot internals. Mirrors §11.O's defaultPlotAppender posture documented in mx-bd4d67 and the broader plot-client facade narrowing — warren never lets a Plot outage take down a run or PlanRun lifecycle transition.

content: Ghost-run reconciliation (warren-b1a9): bridgeRunStream catches BurrowNotFoundError from the source iterator and returns burrowRunMissing:true (distinct from errored:true so it does NOT trigger reconnect). createBridgeRegistry's runWithReconnect handles burrowRunMissing by calling reconcileLostBurrowRun (src/server/bridges.ts) which transitions the row to failed with failure_reason='burrow_run_lost' (mirroring reap's queued->markRunning->finalize shim for queued ghosts) and emits a bridge_lost system event. bootBridges pre-probes each candidate active run via client.http.runs.get before starting its bridge; 404 there skips with reason='burrow_run_lost' and reconciles the row. cancelRun catches the same 404 (cancel intent = terminalize); steerRun catches it as ValidationError (no useful steering target). Adding a new RUN_FAILURE_REASONS member requires editing src/db/schema/columns.ts AND src/ui/src/api/types.ts (UI does NOT import server schema; the boundary is the HTTP wire).

name: usage-hydrate-on-read
description: src/runs/usage-hydrate.ts (warren-ab18) is the read-time fallback that recomputes cost/token totals from persisted events for terminal runs whose bridge died before its next attachStats checkpoint (machine reboot, ghost run). hydrateRunsUsage batches all candidates into one EventsRepo.listUsageEvents call, then overlays SessionStats onto the RunRow. Wired into listRunsHandler and getRunHandler in src/server/handlers.ts so /runs and /runs/:id show a non-null cost in the UI without backfilling the row. Pure shape-sniffing logic lives in src/runs/usage-aggregate.ts and is shared with the bridge's in-stream extractors so write-time and read-time can't drift.

content: EventsRepo.listUsageEvents(runIds) (src/db/repos/events.ts, warren-ab18) is the targeted scan the read-time cost hydrator uses — filters to kind='state_change' AND stream='system' (the carrier both pi turn_end and claude-code result envelopes ride on) and orders by (runId, seq) so callers can group + aggregate in a single pass. Empty runIds short-circuits without a DB hit.

Bridge usage extraction (accumulatePiUsage/extractClaudeUsage/SessionStatsAccumulator) lives in src/runs/usage-aggregate.ts (warren-ab18) as the canonical shape-sniff. stream.ts imports the same functions for in-stream extraction so write-time (bridge attachStats) and read-time (hydrateRunsUsage) can't drift. Inputs are a structural UsageEventInput {kind, stream, payload}; eventRowToUsageInput adapts EventRow (payloadJson → payload).

name: bridge-run-state-poller-terminal-fallback
description: bridgeRunStream (src/runs/stream.ts, warren-6596) runs a low-frequency burrow-runs.get poller in parallel with the event stream. When the runtime doesn't emit a recognised terminal envelope (raw-text declarative agents like the acceptance stub-shell, claude-code without a result envelope, any future runtime), the poller observes burrow's terminal state (succeeded/failed/cancelled), waits a 1s drain window for tailBurrow to flush final events, then aborts the source. The bridge synthesises terminalDetected from the burrow state (1:1 outcome map) so the registry's inline reap runs exactly as it would have via in-stream terminal-detect. In-stream detection (mx-a91bb9) wins when both fire because it carries exit_code semantics from the runtime parser. Disabled in tests that override source without supplying runStateProbe. Default poll: 2s; drain: 1s. Tunable via runStatePollMs / runStateDrainMs test seams.

name: preview-two-phase-probe
description: launchPreview (src/preview/launch.ts, warren-9b15) splits its readiness loop into two named phases keyed on probeOnce returning a tri-state ('ready' | 'http_response' | 'not_connected'): phase 1 ('connect') waits for any HTTP response under DEFAULT_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MS (5m, override .warren/preview.yaml connect_timeout, 1s..1h bounds in PreviewConnectTimeoutSchema), phase 2 ('readiness') waits for 2xx/3xx under readiness_timeout starting at the phase transition (not sidecar create). Bun fetch resolves with a Response object once headers are in (TCP connected + bytes flowed) → 'http_response' flips to phase 2; throws on transport-level failures (refused, abort) → 'not_connected' keeps the loop in phase 1. preview_failure_message records the failing phase ('phase=connect:' / 'phase=readiness:') and reason='connect_timeout' is a new distinct LaunchFailureReason. reap.ts plumbs previewConfig.connect_timeout through parseDurationMs the same way readiness_timeout/setup_timeout do.

content: stagePlotForCommit (src/runs/reap.ts, warren-343a) is reap's 4th-and-a-half best-effort sub-step, sandwiched between mergePlot and branch_push. Reads from project.localPath/.plot/ (the union point after mergePlot), filters to plot-*.{events.jsonl,json} (skips .index.db* and non-plot-* entries), copies into workspace/.plot/, runs 'git add -- .plot/', then 'git diff --cached --quiet -- .plot/' to detect a staged delta. On non-zero exit (staged changes present) authors 'chore(warren): plot state' with -c user.name=warren -c user.email=warren@os-eco.dev. Gated on project.hasPlot — projects without .plot/ are byte-identical to pre-warren-343a reap. Emits reap.plot_committed when a commit fires. Failures emit reap_failed step=plot_commit and never fail the run. Result surface adds plotCommitted: boolean.

fakeExec (src/runs/reap.test.ts, warren-343a) gained a 'stagedDelta?: boolean' opt that toggles whether 'git diff --cached --quiet ...' throws (non-zero exit = staged changes). Default false (exits zero = no staged delta). Used by warren-343a plot_commit tests to flip the has-staged-delta branch of stagePlotForCommit without instrumenting the full git plumbing.

title: Use workspace as canonical .plot/ commit target (not project clone)
rationale: branch_push (reap.ts ~line 482) pushes from workspacePath, which burrow cloned fresh from origin. Host-side appenders (defaultPlotAppender etc.) write to project.localPath/.plot/ — those writes never reach the workspace's HEAD, so committing in project.localPath would not ship them to origin. Chose to replicate project→workspace at reap-time (after mergePlot which writes workspace→project for project-side observability) and commit+push from workspace. This piggy-backs on the existing branch_push primitive, requires no new push surface, and keeps the project clone as the union point for snapshot+restore (warren-fdd2 shape b) while the workspace stays as the canonical ship-to-origin source.

content: src/runs/interactive.ts (warren-1117 / pl-0344 step 3) is the respawn-per-turn primitive: spawnInteractiveTurn(runId, message) takes a prior interactive run as the conversation handle, loads Plot context (intent + attachments + last N events) via the PlotContextReader seam, composes a structured <plot_context>+<user_message> prompt, and forwards to spawnRun with mode='interactive'. Each turn is a NEW warren run row (not row-mutation) so spawnRun's machinery (project refresh, agent rendering, PLOT_ID env injection, run_dispatched Plot append) is inherited verbatim. user_message events land on the NEW turn's run id with seq via events.maxSeqForRun+1. Plot-context load failure is best-effort: emits plot_context_load_failed system event but does NOT block dispatch. Required spawnRun extension: mode?: RunMode forwarded to repos.runs.create. Constants INTERACTIVE_USER_MESSAGE_KIND='user_message' and INTERACTIVE_AGENT_MESSAGE_KIND='agent_message' are the canonical event-kind strings the supervisor pause detector (warren-2976) and reap-side capture reference. appendAgentMessage helper exported for reap to call when run.mode==='interactive' on terminal.

Pause detector (src/runs/pause.ts, warren-2976 / pl-0344 step 5) is the load-bearing primitive for blocking-question pauses on batch runs. tickPauseDetector() lists running batch runs with plot_id, opens UserPlotClient via the PlotEventReader seam, and on an unanswered question_posed transitions running→paused via repos.runs.markPaused() with paused_at + paused_question_event_id stamped. Resume pass on paused rows checks for question_answered (data.question_id match) or pauseTimeoutMs elapsed since paused_at — both flip paused→running via markResumedFromPause (clears both pause cols) and call the respawn seam. Emits pause.detected / pause.resumed / pause.timed_out system events on the run. Interactive runs are skipped (they own their own respawn lifecycle). bootPauseDetector wraps it with the single-flight tick wrapper mirroring bootPlanRunCoordinator (mx-985743). Default-disabled at boot — enable via WARREN_PAUSE_DETECTOR_ENABLED=1.

name: scenario-32-plot-workbench-loop
description: Acceptance harness scenario 32 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/32-plot-workbench-loop.ts, warren-7cd9 / pl-0344 step 16) exercises the full V1+V1.5 Plot-workbench loop end-to-end against a live warren+burrow: POST /brainstorm spawns the first interactive turn, POST /runs/:id/messages chains a follow-up, POST /plots/:id/formalize returns the legitimate source_message_count:0 envelope, intent edit + status drafting->ready->active promotes, then a batch run pauses on a host-injected question_posed (the burrow-cli body.env forwarding gap from warren-a346 is sidestepped by appending via the host-side 'plot' CLI on project.localPath; the pause detector polls events.jsonl directly), POST /plots/:id/questions/:event_id/answer drives the resume tick, batch reaches succeeded, gh_pr attach + POST /plots/:id/attachments/:ref/merge (WARREN_GH_FETCH_OVERRIDE=merged shim) returns merge.kind='merged' + refresh_scheduled=true, active->done, GET /plots/:id/summary renders the curated artifact (linked_prs/linked_commits/linked_seeds/decisions/timeline arrays; field is linked_prs NOT pull_requests). Per-scenario in-proc stack with WARREN_PAUSE_DETECTOR_ENABLED=1 + WARREN_PAUSE_DETECTOR_TICK_MS=500. Stub batch agent sleeps 18s via [sleep_ms=...]; answer happens mid-sleep so reap lands running->succeeded (paused->succeeded is illegal per ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS).

Interactive canopy agents (brainstorm/planner) failed reap as 'failed/never_started' in <1s because src/runs/spawn.ts dispatched POST /burrows/:id/runs with agentId = agent.name. Burrow's BUILT_IN_RUNTIMES only knows claude-code/sapling/pi/codex; brainstorm/planner are warren-side canopy definitions meant to layer on top of a runtime, not register as their own. Hidden by accident: prior built-ins (claude-code/sapling/pi) all had name==runtime, so the bug only surfaced when the Plot brainstorming UI fired the first agent with a non-runtime name.
resolution: warren-ebca: introduce frontmatter.runtime + readRuntimeId() in src/registry/schema.ts; stamp runtime: 'claude-code' on BRAINSTORM_BUILTIN and PLANNER_BUILTIN; route dispatchRun's agentId through readRuntimeId(agent). Falls back to agent.name so claude-code/sapling/pi are untouched.

description: Warren's dispatchRun in src/runs/spawn.ts:392 passes agent.name as the burrow runtime id on POST /burrows/:id/runs. Burrow's BUILT_IN_RUNTIMES (burrow/src/runtime/registry.ts:24) is hardcoded to claude-code/sapling/codex/pi only. Any warren-side canopy/builtin agent whose name doesn't match one of those four runtimes fails dispatch with errorMessage='agent X is not registered' and finalizes as failed/never_started. Hit first by the brainstorm/planner interactive builtins introduced for the Plot brainstorming UI (pl-0344) — fly logs surface the errorMessage but the warren-side run only shows failure_reason=never_started, so root cause requires correlating burrow_run_id across the two log streams.
resolution: Tracked in warren-ebca: add optional frontmatter.runtime field to AgentDefinition, stamp runtime='claude-code' on BRAINSTORM_BUILTIN and PLANNER_BUILTIN, and change dispatchRun callers to send agent.frontmatter.runtime ?? agent.name. claude-code/sapling/pi keep working via the name=runtime fallback.

description: spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts) called provisionBurrow with agent.name as the agentId but dispatchRun used readRuntimeId(agent) — divergent. For canopy agents whose name doesn't match a burrow runtime (e.g. brainstorm, planner with frontmatter.runtime='claude-code'), burrow's POST /burrows got agents:['planner'], registry.get('planner') returned undefined, collectToolchainPaths returned [] for that agent, no toolchain ro-bind for the claude binary, and bwrap fails 'execvp claude: No such file or directory' ~17s into the run. Manifests as failed/no_model_response with written=0/skipped=1 in the bridge log (the single stderr line was the bwrap exec error). Pi-runtime agents (canopy name='pi') worked because name matched the runtime id.
resolution: Pass readRuntimeId(agent) to provisionBurrow at src/runs/spawn.ts:376 so the burrow up call's agents[] carries the runtime id (e.g. 'claude-code'), not the canopy agent name. Burrow then resolves the runtime, runs installCheck, and ro-binds the binary's dir into the sandbox. Test guard added in spawn.test.ts under the warren-ebca brainstorm-runtime case: asserts the POST /burrows body's agents = ['claude-code'].

content: stageSeedsForCommit (src/runs/reap.ts, warren-7ecc) is reap's commit-through-reap step 4.5b — mirror of stagePlotForCommit (warren-343a, mx-9f06df) gated on project.hasSeeds. Replicates SEEDS_COMMITTABLE_FILES (issues.jsonl + plans.jsonl only — config.yaml + templates.jsonl are human-managed at sd init time) from project clone into burrow workspace, stages .seeds/, and authors a 'chore(warren): seeds state' commit when 'git diff --cached --quiet -- .seeds/' returns non-zero. Closes the planner-default-prompt bug: planner's system prompt forbids git commit, so sd plan submit writes .seeds/issues.jsonl + .seeds/plans.jsonl inside the workspace; without this step push lands empty (reap.empty_push fires) and the plan is lost. ReapRunResult.seedsCommitted carries the outcome; reap.completed payload exposes seeds.committed: boolean. Failures emit reap_failed step=seeds_commit and never fail the run.

RunsRepo.listAll/listByProject/listByAgent (src/db/repos/runs.ts, warren-ee50 / pl-b0c0 step 1) accept {limit, offset, sort, dir} \u2014 offset was added so the Runs page can paginate past the historical 100-row cap. Pair with RunsRepo.aggregate({projectId?, agentName?}) which returns {total, costTotalUsd, costPricedCount} for the SAME predicate the list*() siblings page over (uses sql`count(*)` + sql`sum(cost_usd)` + sql`count(cost_usd)` for NULLS-aware pricing rollup). Ghost runs whose cost is only recoverable via hydrateRunsUsage in-stream extraction are NOT folded in \u2014 deliberate single-DB-pass cheap-header trade-off.

name: cost-analytics-pure-aggregator
description: src/runs/cost-analytics.ts (warren-cf63 / pl-b0c0 step 6) is the pure pipeline behind GET /analytics/cost. buildCostAnalytics(rows) takes a flat CostAnalyticsRow[] (one per runs row, with provider/model already extracted from renderedAgentJson.frontmatter and planId already resolved via plan_run_children join) and emits eight breakdowns: date (chronological), project/plan/plot/run/agent/model/provider (sorted cost desc, ties broken by runs desc then key asc). Null group keys fold into a NONE_KEY ('__none__') bucket per dimension, rendered as em-dash in the UI so unattributed spend stays visible. costUsd=null counts toward 'runs' and 'priced' but adds 0 to the sum, mirroring RunsRepo.aggregate's NULLS-aware rollup (mx-2ae43b). Keep the pure split so write-time aggregation and the handler can't drift.

name: mirrorSeeds propagates new seed rows
description: mirrorSeeds (formerly mirrorClosedSeeds) propagates ALL new seed rows from the burrow workspace to the project clone, not just closed ones. New rows (IDs absent from the project clone) are added regardless of status; existing rows are only updated when closed with a newer updatedAt. This fixes planner runs whose sd plan submit creates open children that were silently dropped at reap. ReapResult gained a seedsCreated field alongside seedsClosed.

name: auto_plan_run reap hook
description: auto_plan_run reap hook (warren-a32a): when agent frontmatter has auto_plan_run:true and outcome=succeeded, reap diffs workspace vs project .seeds/plans.jsonl to detect new plans and auto-dispatches a plan-run per new plan via repos.planRuns.create(). Workspace plans body is cached BEFORE stageSeedsForCommit (which copies project→workspace and would overwrite the agent's version). Detection is gated on project.hasSeeds + outcome=succeeded + frontmatter check. Non-fatal: errors emit reap_failed step=auto_plan_run. Result extends ReapRunResult with autoPlanRunCreated/autoPlanRunId/autoPlanRunPlanId.

content: resolveAutoPlanRunAgent (src/runs/reap.ts, warren-65b2) reads auto_plan_run_agent from renderedAgentJson.frontmatter to override the parent run's agentName when creating auto-dispatched plan-runs. Falls back to run.agentName when unset. Both bugwatch and nightwatch set auto_plan_run_agent: 'pi' so child runs boot with a coding agent prompt, not the triage prompt.

name: mirrorPlans append-only plans.jsonl mirror
description: mirrorPlans (src/runs/reap.ts, warren-d9a2) is reap's post-mirrorSeeds step that reads .seeds/plans.jsonl from the burrow workspace via burrowClient.http.files.read and appends rows (by id) absent from the project clone. Append-only: existing plans are never overwritten. Without this step, stageSeedsForCommit copies the stale project baseline plans.jsonl back into the workspace, losing agent-created plans from sd plan submit.

content: spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts, warren-5797) injects WARREN_QUALITY_GATE into the burrow env when the project's .warren/config.yaml declares qualityGate. composeRunEnv merges Plot identity vars and quality-gate into one env block. Coding agent built-ins (claude-code/pi/sapling) instruct agents to check $WARREN_QUALITY_GATE before committing.

detectRuntimeTerminal (src/runs/stream.ts) maps pi agent_end → failed when the payload carries stopReason === "error" OR a non-empty errorMessage (warren-1ac2 / pl-5516 step 1). Mirrors the claude-code result.is_error guard so provider outages (overloaded_error 529, rate_limit, network) surface as failed runs instead of silent-success. Zero-tokens / empty-content alone is NOT a failure signal — a legitimate noop run shares that shape; require an explicit error marker. isPiAgentEnd is unchanged and still returns true for both happy + error agent_end events so the piStats terminal snapshot still fires. Supersedes the earlier always-succeeded mapping (mx-a91bb9).

detectRuntimeTerminal (src/runs/stream.ts) is exported (warren-6fcc / pl-5516 step 2) so its branch table can be unit-tested directly in src/runs/stream.detect.test.ts without dragging the full bridgeRunStream fixture (db, repos, broker, pool). Bridge-level behavior (terminal breaks the loop, trailing events dropped) stays covered by warren-2687 in src/runs/stream.test.ts.

Handlers monolith split phase 2 (warren-a2b4 / pl-9088 step 2): src/server/handlers/index.ts → runs.ts (789 lines) + plan-runs.ts (484 lines). Pattern matches phase 1 (mx-3df5c5): ROUTE_TABLE stays in handlers/index.ts as the canonical wire surface for gen:docs / gen:openapi; shared helpers (readJsonBody[OrEmpty], requireString, optionalString, requireParam, parseBoolean, parseNonNegativeInt, assertPlotIdDispatchable, defaultSpawn) are exported from index.ts and cross-imported. Streaming plumbing (bridgeAbort/asNdjsonStream/eventToNdjson) lives in runs.ts and is re-exported for plan-runs.ts since both NDJSON tails share it — keeping it adjacent to streamRunEventsHandler (the larger consumer) rather than hoisting to index.ts. Tests: relocated handlers.plan-runs.test.ts → handlers/plan-runs.test.ts and removed its biome noExcessiveLinesPerFunction override by splitting the monolithic POST /plan-runs describe into three siblings (core + Plot integration (warren-b89f) + plot_id validation (warren-bae5)) sharing a setupPlanRunFixture() factory. Helpers (poolFor, depsFor, makeBurrowClient) are duplicated between runs.test.ts and the residual handlers.test.ts because both files retain describes that use them — splitting helpers into a shared module would have leaked test-only plumbing across multiple files.

reap.ts monolith split (warren-ea0c / pl-9088 step 4): src/runs/reap.ts (2114 lines) + reap.test.ts (2792 lines) → src/runs/reap/ subdirectory with per-sub-step modules. Pattern mirrors handlers split (mx-3df5c5/mx-99ad0d/mx-929bbc): index.ts is the re-export shim + module-level doc-comment; types.ts pulls the public interfaces into their own file so they can be imported without dragging the orchestrator's transitive imports; run.ts holds the reapRun orchestrator after extracting three helpers (runPreviewLaunch + runPreviewAnnotate in preview.ts, runPrOpen in pr-open.ts, dispatchAutoPlanRuns in auto-plan-run.ts) to fit under the 500-line threshold; pure helpers (mergeMulchFile, mergePlotEventsFile, mergePlotJsonFile) re-export through index.ts so external consumers (src/runs/index.ts) only change the import path suffix. Tests split alongside: each .test.ts sits next to its module and references shared fixtures via reap/test-helpers.ts (fakeFs, fakeExec, fakeBurrowClient, fakeOpenPr, setup, makePool, makeBurrow, Ctx + re-exported bun-side types). The four complex functions still over Biome's noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity ceiling (mergeMulch, mergePlot, mirrorSeeds, mirrorPlans, reapRun) carry over to the override block under their new module paths — refactoring those algorithms was explicitly out of scope for the mechanical split.

Split src/plan-runs/coordinator.test.ts (804 lines) into coordinator.test-helpers.ts and four focused per-phase test files (coordinator.dispatch.test.ts, coordinator.gating.test.ts, coordinator.resume.test.ts, coordinator.completion.test.ts) under 500 lines to remove its file-size budget

runWorkspaceDestroy (src/runs/reap/destroy.ts, warren-0d89) is reap's final sub-step: after the terminal-state transition + reap.completed emit, it calls client.http.burrows.destroy(burrowId,{archive:true}) via withTransportMapping, deletes the warren burrows row (repos.burrows.delete), and emits reap.workspace_destroyed {burrowId,archived,deletedEvents,deletedMessages,deletedRuns}. Best-effort like the other sub-steps (fail()->reap_failed step=workspace_destroy, never blocks the run). Skips (emits reap.workspace_destroy_skipped) for interactive runs and live/starting previews (previewLaunchState==='live' or run.previewState in starting/live); skips silently when burrowId or workerClient is null. Surfaces workspaceDestroyed:boolean on ReapRunResult. Fixes the 2026-05-27 disk-full incident where 122 stale burrow workspaces filled the Fly volume. The DELETE /burrows/:id wire response is DestroyBurrowResult (no workspaceRemoved field — that lives only on burrow's in-process destroyBurrowFully), so the event reports archived as a boolean + deleted counts.

All warren-authored reap-time bookkeeping commits now pass --no-verify so project git hooks (e.g. a pre-commit running the full check:all gauntlet) never block them. Covered: src/runs/reap/stage.ts (chore(warren): plot state, chore(warren): seeds state) and src/plots/sync.ts (plot sync: update plot metadata). --no-verify sits immediately after the 'commit' subcommand arg. Agent-authored commits inside the sandbox are deliberately NOT bypassed (warren-27d3).

warren-88da / pl-369d: the 'functions coverage drop below 85%' bug no longer reproduces — sibling step warren-f65e already ratcheted floors up to functions 87.09 / lines 90.32 after coverage was restored by the handlers/stream/preview splits. Current coverage is functions 87.62 / lines 90.83 (both green). Hardened the resolution by covering both exported functions in src/runs/stream/terminal-detect.ts (was 50% func / 63% lines): added isPiAgentEnd tests plus the claude-code result branch and null/non-object payload guards, lifting that module to 100/100.

buildRunMetrics (src/runs/analytics/run-metrics.ts, warren-368e / pl-ad0f step 1) is the pure run-level analytics aggregator mirroring cost-analytics.ts: takes RunMetricsRow[] (one per runs row, w/ provider/model pre-extracted from frontmatter + seedId) and emits totals (run/state counts, successRate over terminal runs only, avg/median/p95 durationMs via nearest-rank percentile, contextTokens + cost StatSummaries), timeSeries (per-day buckets w/ per-state counts, NONE_KEY for null startedAt sorted last), byAgent/byModel/byProvider RunGroupBuckets ranked by total context desc, byFailureReason (failed runs only), topSeedsByContext (excludes null seedId per risk #4). Conventions: contextTokens = tokensInput + tokensCacheRead (null only when BOTH null, excluded from averages not counted as 0 per risk #3); durationMsOf returns null unless both timestamps valid + non-negative; NONE_KEY='__none__' folds null group keys. Step 2 (warren-0692) wires the GET /analytics/runs handler.

name: fallback-workspace-gc
description: Fallback workspace GC (warren-0a9a): src/runs/reap/gc.ts sweeps the burrows placement table for rows whose runs are ALL terminal and whose newest activity (latest terminal endedAt, else burrows.added_at) is older than WARREN_WORKSPACE_GC_TTL (default 1h), then calls client.http.burrows.destroy(archive:true) + repos.burrows.delete per row. Complements per-reap workspace_destroy (warren-0d89) for crash-mid-reap / force-killed cases. Pure predicate findStrandedBurrows + buildBurrowActivity(activeRuns,terminalRuns) reduce listByState(queued/running/paused) and listByState(succeeded/failed/cancelled) rows — NO new RunsRepo aggregate methods (avoids growing runs.ts past its frozen 610-line file-size budget; first attempt added activeBurrowIds()/latestEndedAtByBurrow() and blew the ratchet). Worker startWorkspaceGcWorker mirrors startPreviewEvictionWorker: single-flight, NOOP_HANDLE when disabled, stop() drains in-flight. Wired in src/server/main/index.ts (config + worker + teardown); config threaded as ServerDeps.workspaceGcTtlMs. Shared findStrandedBurrows backs the stale_burrow_workspaces diagnostic in src/diagnostics/stale-workspaces.ts (separate file, NOT checks.ts which is at its 616-line frozen budget) surfaced via warren doctor + GET /readyz.

name: run-replicate-clone-from
description: Re-run/clone (warren-e96f): POST /runs accepts cloneFromRunId to replicate a prior run's agent/model/project/prompt against the project default base. Shares runs.parent_run_id with continuation (warren-4b11) but discriminates via the new runs.clone_kind enum ('replicate' vs 'continue'). spawnRun's resolveContinuationRef branches on input.cloneKind: 'replicate' returns the explicit ref (not the parent's pushed branch); default/'continue' recomposes burrow/<parentId>. Handler derives effective provider/model via readProviderFrontmatter off the parent's frozen rendered_agent_json so the replica fires onto the exact same model.

content: composeRunBranch (src/runs/branch.ts) takes an optional 3rd arg targetBranch (warren-05ea): a non-empty/non-whitespace value short-circuits the ${prefix}/${runId} composition so a CI-fixer run pushes to the existing PR branch; empty/whitespace falls back to the composed branch so a stray config never strands a spawn on a blank ref.

title: Plan-run coordinator fails terminally on a definitive missing-seed (warren-0fed)
rationale: An infinite retryable noop on a not-found child seed spammed plan_run.noop and wedged plan-runs in 'running' forever; a definitive not-found is unrecoverable so it must be terminal, but transient sd failures (timeout/lock) must stay retryable so a hung seed store doesn't kill healthy plan-runs.

name: auto_plan_run child-seed validation
description: auto_plan_run reap path (src/runs/reap/auto-plan-run.ts dispatchAutoPlanRuns, warren-41d5) now validates each new plan's child seeds via showSeed before creating a plan-run, mirroring the manual POST /plan-runs handler: SeedNotFoundError on any child -> skip the plan + emit auto_plan_run_skipped {planId, reason:'missing_child_seeds', missing}; all-children-closed -> skip with reason 'all_children_closed'; transient sd errors propagate to fail('auto_plan_run'). Gated on an optional seedsCli?: SeedsCliDeps seam threaded ReapRunInput -> run.ts -> CreateBridgeRegistryInput (bridges.ts) -> RunWithReconnectInput (bridge-reconnect.ts), wired in main/index.ts bootBridges (reusing the coordinator's {sdBinary: schedulerConfig.sdBinary, spawn: defaultSpawn}) + cli/commands/run.ts. Absent seedsCli = no validation (legacy unit tests unchanged). Depends on warren-0fed's SeedNotFoundError discriminator.

name: run-heartbeat-watchdog
description: Run heartbeat watchdog (warren-285d): src/runs/watchdog.ts force-fails 'running' runs that go silent-but-busy (e.g. a runaway quality-gate command behind a stuck bash tool — biome pinning cores, exitCode null, no new events). tickWatchdog scans repos.runs.listByState('running'), computes idleMs = now - newest-event-ts (computeIdleMs falls back to startedAt; null when no parseable anchor), and when idleMs >= heartbeatTimeoutMs runs forceFail: emit watchdog.timed_out system event (WATCHDOG_TIMED_OUT_KIND), best-effort POST /runs/:burrow_run_id/cancel (swallows BurrowNotFoundError + transport errors), then reapRun({outcome:'failed', failureReason:'timed_out'}). Routing through reap (not bare finalize) is deliberate: reap's workspace_destroy sub-step destroys the burrow workspace which tears down the bwrap process tree (the cancel-doesn't-reap-tree gap), and reap still pushes partial work — same philosophy as cancelRun (warren-a69a). Opt-in via WARREN_RUN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS>0 (loadWatchdogConfigFromEnv; tick WARREN_WATCHDOG_TICK_MS default 30000). bootWatchdog mirrors bootPauseDetector single-flight wrapper. Wired in src/server/main/detector-wiring.ts (bootWatchdogFromEnv + bootPauseDetectorFromEnv extracted from index.ts to stay under the 500-line file-size budget); teardown order planRunCoordinator -> pauseDetector -> watchdog -> scheduler. 'timed_out' was already a reserved RUN_FAILURE_REASONS value (src/db/schema/columns.ts) awaiting this deadline-based reaper.

content: warren-c770 / LEVERET.md §0.4: the pi-chat runtime suppresses the per-turn agent_end terminal envelope, so a conversation run stays non-terminal across turns. Three warren-side lifetime guards gained an explicit conversation skip: (1) heartbeat watchdog (src/runs/watchdog.ts) — 'if (run.mode==="conversation") continue' before computeIdleMs so an armed WARREN_RUN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS can't force-fail idle conversations; (2) reap workspace-destroy (src/runs/reap/destroy.ts) — extends the interactive exemption to conversation, emitting reap.workspace_destroy_skipped reason 'conversation_run'; (3) bootBridges crash-recovery (src/server/bridges.ts) — on a pre-probe 404 for a conversation run, skip the bridge with reason 'conversation_burrow_lost' WITHOUT calling reconcileLostBurrowRun, leaving the row non-terminal so re-wake (warren-6ccf) replays the transcript. Pause detector (pause.ts guards mode!=='batch') and workspace GC (state-keyed) already exempt conversation for free. Also added 'conversation' to RUN_MODES in src/db/schema/columns.ts.

name: conversation-rewake-seam-primitive
description: Leveret build-phase-2 lifetime/re-wake primitives (conversation-idle.ts, conversation-rewake.ts) are landed as pure orchestration + seam interfaces (reader/rotator/spawner) BEFORE the warren-0b91 conversations/messages tables exist. rewakeConversation() takes a ConversationRewakeReader + ConversationAnchorRotator seam and an injectable RewakeSpawner (defaults to spawnRun), so unit tests stay disk/table-free; production repo-backed impls slot in at the HTTP boundary (warren-af15). Re-wake refuses non-active conversations and non-terminal anchoring runs, replays the full DB transcript verbatim via buildRewakePrompt into a fresh mode:'conversation' spawnRun, rotates anchoring_run_id, then emits conversation.rewake_replayed. No pi --session resume / old burrow dependency.

warren-ce65 (conversation stream-bridge: persist turns to messages + parse propose_intent from tool_execution_end + write intent_edited(actor=agent:leveret) host-side) is hard-blocked by two unmet dependencies as of 2026-06-06. (1) warren-0b91 NOT implemented: no conversations/messages tables in src/db/schema/sqlite.ts and no MessagesRepo/ConversationsRepo in src/db/repos/, so message-turn persistence is impossible. (2) The ../plot ACL widening (EVENT_ACL.intent_edited -> ['user','agent']) NOT shipped: published @os-eco/plot-cli@0.4.0 still pins intent_edited:['user'] (asserted in its own acl.test.ts) and assertCanEmit rejects agents ('agents must surface a question_posed event instead of mutating intent'). warren's facade also still lists intent_edited in HUMANS_ONLY_EVENT_TYPES (src/plot-client/types.ts). An agent:leveret intent_edited write throws at both facade and lib runtime. The plot repo is not co-mounted (no ../plot; origin is warren only) so the ACL cannot be widened from this run.
resolution: Leave ce65 open. Sequence: (a) land warren-0b91 first (tables+repos), (b) ship the ACL widening in ../plot and publish a plot-cli bump, (c) pin the bump in package.json + remove intent_edited from HUMANS_ONLY_EVENT_TYPES in src/plot-client/types.ts and add editIntent/agent-intent surface to AgentPlotHandle, (d) then build the conversation bridge variant. No fabricated partial committed because any wired slice would either fail check:deps (unused export) or throw at the ACL boundary.

name: Send-off merge poller (warren-b872)
description: src/runs/conversation-merge-poller.ts polls closed conversations carrying a submitted_pr_url whose planner_run_id is still null (ConversationsRepo.listAwaitingPlannerDispatch), reuses src/plan-runs/pr-merge.ts createPrMergeChecker for retry-aware merge probes, and on merge auto-dispatches the planner keyed on plot_id via the PlannerDispatchFn seam. recordPlannerDispatch is a single-shot guard (writes only when planner_run_id IS NULL, returns null on a second call) so a re-detect never double-dispatches. Boot wrapper bootConversationMergePoller mirrors bootPauseDetector single-flight; createMergePollerDispatch (conversation-merge-dispatch.ts) wraps spawnRun+bridges.start like createPlanRunSpawn. Opt-in via WARREN_MERGE_POLLER_ENABLED=1, tick WARREN_MERGE_POLLER_TICK_MS (default 30s).

title: Retired mode=interactive / brainstorm path (warren-d622)
rationale: LEVERET.md §0.8 last-phase cleanup: intent shaping migrated to conversations (steerRun channel); keeping the dead respawn-per-turn primitive was pure liability once conversation parity landed.

description: warren-005d's coordinator (PR #266) landed src/runs/conversation-idle.ts + the conversation.idleTimeoutMs knob fully tested but NEVER BOOTED — bootConversationIdleDetector had no production caller, so idle conversation runs would run forever. When a seed ships a 'boot-worker', verify the wiring in src/server/main/detector-wiring.ts + bootServer stop chain, not just the module.
resolution: Added createRepoIdleConversationReader (repo-backed reader: active conversations joined against a running anchoring run) co-located in conversation-idle.ts mirroring defaultPlotEventReader, plus bootConversationIdleDetectorFromEnv in detector-wiring.ts — ON by default (lifecycle reclaim like preview eviction / workspace GC, NOT opt-in like pause/watchdog/merge-poller), opt-out WARREN_CONVERSATION_IDLE_DISABLED=1, tick WARREN_CONVERSATION_IDLE_TICK_MS default 60s; handle stopped in bootServer teardown.

name: PR-open retry with injectable sleep seam
description: warren-70c6: openPullRequest (src/runs/pr.ts) embeds errors[] JSON in the message for non-already-exists 422 failures so operators can diagnose transient vs permanent errors. runPrOpen (src/runs/reap/pr-open.ts) wraps tryOpenPr in a bounded retry loop (3 retries, delays 1s/2s/4s) for http_error results that are not the permanent 'no commits between' 422 shape. A sleep seam (RunPrOpenInput.sleep) is threaded from ReapRunInput through run.ts so tests inject a no-op and avoid real delays. isRetryablePrResult returns false for missing_token and network errors (surface immediately) and for permanent 422s.

content: warren-5e53: reapRun short-circuits the workspace pipeline when stateOnEntry === 'queued' (agent never ran). Queued runs now emit reap.never_started_skip and skip mulch/seeds/plot-merge/plot-commit/branch-push, proceeding only to state transition + reap.completed + workspace destroy. Guard is an if/else-if split at the workspacePath+project block in src/runs/reap/run.ts.

name: Conversation run keep-alive in the stream bridge
description: warren-df71: mode:'conversation' runs are long-lived pi-chat sessions, so the stream bridge must NOT treat pi agent_end as run-terminal. Thread RunMode through bridges.start -> runWithReconnect -> bridgeRunStream; when mode==='conversation' the bridge treats agent_end as a TURN boundary (persist usage, flush accumulated assistant text via ConversationTurnHandler.persistAssistantTurn, apply propose_intent patches, then continue without break/terminalDetected). Non-conversation lifecycles unchanged because the whole branch is gated on mode. New module src/runs/stream/conversation-turn.ts owns the side effects + pure parsers extractAssistantText/extractIntentPatch.

auto_plan_run frontmatter string/boolean trap (warren-5f07): cn --fm key:value stringifies every value, so a canopy-authored agent setting auto_plan_run: true lands in rendered_agent_json as the string "true" — hasAutoPlanRunFrontmatter in src/runs/reap/auto-plan-run.ts checked === true and silently lost auto-dispatch.
resolution: coerceBooleanFlag() accepts real boolean true OR case-insensitive trimmed string "true"; "false"/other => false. Apply the same defensive coercion to any boolean-ish frontmatter flag read from rendered_agent_json. Upstream fix tracked as warren-6060 (typed frontmatter --fm key:=value JSON syntax).

content: Warren-authored bookkeeping commits (reap 'chore(warren): plot/seeds state' in src/runs/reap/stage.ts; plot-sync commit in src/plots/sync.ts) author as the canonical bot identity warren <warren@os-eco.dev> via WARREN_BOT_IDENTITY / warrenCommitIdentityArgs() in src/bot-identity.ts (warren-598f). Never re-spell user.name/user.email inline at a new commit site — import the helper so git history can't drift (Article VII). Distinct from the agent's own operator-configured author identity installed by src/supervisor/git-identity.ts via WARREN_GIT_AUTHOR_NAME/EMAIL.

name: Per-run spend caps (warren-a63d)
description: maxCostUsd is enforced as a single effective cap frozen onto runs.rendered_agent_json.frontmatter. Precedence trigger > agent: dispatch folds the triggers.yaml per-trigger maxCostUsd over the agent's own frontmatter cap via withMaxCostUsdOverride (src/registry/schema.ts) before freezing the run row, so the bridge resolves ONE cap with resolveCostCapUsd (src/runs/cost-cap.ts). Enforcement is mid-run in the event bridge: as pi turn_end cost accumulates, enforceBudgetCap (src/runs/stream/budget.ts) persists usage + emits a budget.exceeded event + cancels the burrow run, and the bridge breaks with terminalDetected cancelled. Caps fail OPEN (coerceCostCap: malformed/non-positive/NaN -> null) and tolerate cn --fm string stringification (numeric strings accepted), mirroring the warren-5f07 string trap.

content: spawnRun (src/runs/spawn/dispatch.ts, warren-c1a4/pl-fb43 step 3) splits project identity: input.projectId is the execution repo cloned into the burrow workspace; optional input.seedProjectId (default=projectId) is the coordination host clone targeting the post-dispatch seed updateExtensions stamp AND run_dispatched Plot append. resolveCoordinationProject (src/runs/spawn/util.ts) returns the execution project when seedProjectId is unset/empty/equal (byte-identical same-repo path), else repos.projects.require(seedProjectId). Burrow provisioning path is untouched (always execution projectId).

Dispatch-prompt builder (src/runs/dispatch-prompt.ts) is the shared prompt renderer for the plan-run coordinator and (future) single-run dispatch. buildDispatchPrompt({template, seed:{id,title?,body?}, crossRepo?}) substitutes {seed_id} -> seed.id and {seed_body} -> formatSeedContent (title + blank line + body, each trimmed). crossRepo=true (seedProjectId !== execution projectId) auto-appends an injection block with the seed text even when the template only references {seed_id}, so a cross-repo executor needs no in-workspace .seeds/ access; crossRepo=false (default) keeps {seed_id}-only behaviour byte-identical to the legacy template.replace. SEED_BODY_TOKEN is a /g regex so .test() advances lastIndex — reset to 0 before .replace. Seed title+body resolved host-side from sd show --json: SeedShowIssueSchema (src/seeds-cli/schema.ts) now types optional title + description fields; coordinator.ts maps seedShow.description -> seed.body. coordinator.ts is frozen at the 587-line check:size budget, so keep edits there compact.

title: Run heartbeat watchdog is on by default
rationale: A silent-but-busy runaway run (100% CPU bun process behind a stuck tool) sat 'running' for ~2h because the watchdog was opt-in and warren-deployed never set WARREN_RUN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS. A lifecycle-reclaim safety net must not depend on an operator remembering an env var.

content: spawnRun (src/runs/spawn/dispatch.ts, warren-c686/pl-f700 step 1) is instrumented via helpers in src/runs/spawn/rollback.ts: logPlacement/logProvisioned/logDispatched/logSpawnFailed + bindRunLogger(run_id), and rollback() now logs the previously-swallowed runs.finalize and burrow-destroy failures. Events: spawn.placement (pre-row, no run_id yet), spawn.provisioned/dispatched (with duration_ms), spawn.failed (warn), spawn.rollback.finalize_failed, spawn.rollback.burrow_destroy_failed (error). SpawnRunInput.logger is optional (pino-shaped SpawnLogger in types.ts, NOOP_SPAWN_LOGGER fallback); the three HTTP callers (runs/dispatch.ts, conversations.ts, projects.ts) pass ctx.logger which is request_id pre-bound (warren-30af). Logging helpers + rollback were extracted into rollback.ts specifically to keep dispatch.ts under the 500-line check:size ratchet.
